functions.name: Random
doc: Get an integer between 0 and nMaxInteger-1.
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: PrintString
doc: Output sString to the log file.

functions.name: PrintFloat
functions.params: float fFloat, int nWidth = 18, int nDecimals = 9
functions.signature: void PrintFloat(float fFloat, int nWidth = 18, int nDecimals = 9)
doc: Output a formatted float to the log file.
- nWidth should be a value from 0 to 18 inclusive.
- nDecimals should be a value from 0 to 9 inclusive.

functions.name: FloatToString
functions.params: float fFloat, int nWidth = 18, int nDecimals = 9
functions.signature: string FloatToString(float fFloat, int nWidth = 18, int nDecimals = 9)
doc: Convert fFloat into a string.
- nWidth should be a value from 0 to 18 inclusive.
- nDecimals should be a value from 0 to 9 inclusive.

functions.name: PrintInteger
doc: Output nInteger to the log file.

functions.name: PrintObject
doc: Output oObject's ID to the log file.

functions.name: AssignCommand
doc: Assign aActionToAssign to oActionSubject.
No return value, but if an error occurs, the log file will contain
"AssignCommand failed."
(If the object doesn't exist, nothing happens.)

functions.name: DelayCommand
doc: Delay aActionToDelay by fSeconds.
No return value, but if an error occurs, the log file will contain
"DelayCommand failed.".
It is suggested that functions which create effects should not be used
as parameters to delayed actions.  Instead, the effect should be created in the
script and then passed into the action.  For example:
effect eDamage = EffectDamage(nDamage, DAMAGE_TYPE_MAGICAL);
DelayCommand(fDelay, ApplyEffectToObject(DURATION_TYPE_INSTANT, eDamage, oTarget);

functions.name: ExecuteScript
functions.signature: void ExecuteScript(string sScript, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Make oTarget run sScript and then return execution to the calling script.
If sScript does not specify a compiled script, nothing happens.

functions.name: ClearAllActions
functions.params: int nClearCombatState = FALSE, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF
functions.signature: void ClearAllActions(int nClearCombatState = FALSE, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Clear all the actions of oObject.
No return value, but if an error occurs, the log file will contain
"ClearAllActions failed.".
- nClearCombatState: if true, this will immediately clear the combat state
on a creature, which will stop the combat music and allow them to rest,
engage in dialog, or other actions that they would normally have to wait for.

functions.name: SetFacing
functions.signature: void SetFacing(float fDirection, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Cause oObject to face fDirection.
- fDirection is expressed as anticlockwise degrees from Due East.
DIRECTION_EAST, DIRECTION_NORTH, DIRECTION_WEST and DIRECTION_SOUTH are
predefined. (0.0f=East, 90.0f=North, 180.0f=West, 270.0f=South)

functions.name: SetCalendar
functions.params: int nYear, int nMonth, int nDay
functions.signature: void SetCalendar(int nYear, int nMonth, int nDay)
doc: Set the calendar to the specified date.
- nYear should be from 0 to 32000 inclusive
- nMonth should be from 1 to 12 inclusive
- nDay should be from 1 to 28 inclusive
1) Time can only be advanced forwards; attempting to set the time backwards
will result in no change to the calendar.
2) If values larger than the month or day are specified, they will be wrapped
around and the overflow will be used to advance the next field.
e.g. Specifying a year of 1350, month of 33 and day of 10 will result in
the calender being set to a year of 1352, a month of 9 and a day of 10.

functions.name: SetTime
functions.params: int nHour, int nMinute, int nSecond, int nMillisecond
functions.signature: void SetTime(int nHour, int nMinute, int nSecond, int nMillisecond)
doc: Set the time to the time specified.
- nHour should be from 0 to 23 inclusive
- nMinute should be from 0 to 59 inclusive
- nSecond should be from 0 to 59 inclusive
- nMillisecond should be from 0 to 999 inclusive
1) Time can only be advanced forwards; attempting to set the time backwards
will result in the day advancing and then the time being set to that
specified, e.g. if the current hour is 15 and then the hour is set to 3,
the day will be advanced by 1 and the hour will be set to 3.
2) If values larger than the max hour, minute, second or millisecond are
specified, they will be wrapped around and the overflow will be used to
advance the next field, e.g. specifying 62 hours, 250 minutes, 10 seconds
and 10 milliseconds will result in the calendar day being advanced by 2
and the time being set to 18 hours, 10 minutes, 10 milliseconds.

functions.name: GetCalendarYear
doc: Get the current calendar year.

functions.name: GetCalendarMonth
doc: Get the current calendar month.

functions.name: GetCalendarDay
doc: Get the current calendar day.

functions.name: GetTimeHour
doc: Get the current hour.

functions.name: GetTimeMinute
doc: Get the current minute

functions.name: GetTimeSecond
doc: Get the current second

functions.name: GetTimeMillisecond
doc: Get the current millisecond

functions.name: ActionRandomWalk
doc: The action subject will generate a random location near its current location
and pathfind to it.  ActionRandomwalk never ends, which means it is neccessary
to call ClearAllActions in order to allow a creature to perform any other action
once ActionRandomWalk has been called.
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionRandomWalk failed."

functions.name: ActionMoveToLocation
functions.signature: void ActionMoveToLocation(location lDestination, int bRun = FALSE)
doc: The action subject will move to lDestination.
- lDestination: The object will move to this location.  If the location is
invalid or a path cannot be found to it, the command does nothing.
- bRun: If this is TRUE, the action subject will run rather than walk
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"MoveToPoint failed."

functions.name: ActionMoveToObject
functions.params: object oMoveTo, int bRun = FALSE, float fRange = 1.0f
functions.signature: void ActionMoveToObject(object oMoveTo, int bRun = FALSE, float fRange = 1.0f)
doc: Cause the action subject to move to a certain distance from oMoveTo.
If there is no path to oMoveTo, this command will do nothing.
- oMoveTo: This is the object we wish the action subject to move to
- bRun: If this is TRUE, the action subject will run rather than walk
- fRange: This is the desired distance between the action subject and oMoveTo
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionMoveToObject failed."

functions.name: ActionMoveAwayFromObject
functions.params: object oFleeFrom, int bRun = FALSE, float fMoveAwayRange = 40.0f
functions.signature: void ActionMoveAwayFromObject(object oFleeFrom, int bRun = FALSE, float fMoveAwayRange = 40.0f)
doc: Cause the action subject to move to a certain distance away from oFleeFrom.
- oFleeFrom: This is the object we wish the action subject to move away from.
If oFleeFrom is not in the same area as the action subject, nothing will
happen.
- bRun: If this is TRUE, the action subject will run rather than walk
- fMoveAwayRange: This is the distance we wish the action subject to put
between themselves and oFleeFrom
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionMoveAwayFromObject failed."

functions.name: GetArea
doc: Get the area that oTarget is currently in
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: GetEnteringObject
doc: The value returned by this function depends on the object type of the caller:
1) If the caller is a door it returns the object that last
triggered it.
2) If the caller is a trigger, area of effect, module, area or encounter it
returns the object that last entered it.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID
When used for doors, this should only be called from the OnAreaTransitionClick
event.  Otherwise, it should only be called in OnEnter scripts.

functions.name: GetExitingObject
doc: Get the object that last left the caller.  This function works on triggers,
areas of effect, modules, areas and encounters.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID
Should only be called in OnExit scripts.

functions.name: GetPosition
doc: Get the position of oTarget
Return value on error: vector (0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f)

functions.name: GetFacing
doc: Get the direction in which oTarget is facing, expressed as a float between
Return value on error: -1.0f

functions.name: GetItemPossessor
functions.signature: object GetItemPossessor(object oItem, int bReturnBags = FALSE)
doc: Get the possessor of oItem
- bReturnBags: If TRUE will potentially return a bag container item the item is in, instead of
the object holding the bag. Make sure to check the returning item object type with this flag.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: GetItemPossessedBy
doc: Get the object possessed by oCreature with the tag sItemTag
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: CreateItemOnObject
functions.params: string sItemTemplate, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nStackSize = 1, string sNewTag = ""
functions.signature: object CreateItemOnObject(string sItemTemplate, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nStackSize = 1, string sNewTag = "")
doc: Create an item with the template sItemTemplate in oTarget's inventory.
- nStackSize: This is the stack size of the item to be created
- sNewTag: If this string is not empty, it will replace the default tag from the template
Return value: The object that has been created.  On error, this returns
OBJECT_INVALID.
If the item created was merged into an existing stack of similar items,
the function will return the merged stack object. If the merged stack
overflowed, the function will return the overflowed stack that was created.

functions.name: ActionEquipItem
doc: Equip oItem into nInventorySlot.
- nInventorySlot: INVENTORY_SLOT_*
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionEquipItem failed."
Note:
If the creature already has an item equipped in the slot specified, it will be
unequipped automatically by the call to ActionEquipItem.
In order for ActionEquipItem to succeed the creature must be able to equip the
item oItem normally. This means that:
1) The item is in the creature's inventory.
2) The item must already be identified (if magical).
3) The creature has the level required to equip the item (if magical and ILR is on).
4) The creature possesses the required feats to equip the item (such as weapon proficiencies).

functions.name: ActionUnequipItem
doc: Unequip oItem from whatever slot it is currently in.

functions.name: ActionPickUpItem
doc: Pick up oItem from the ground.
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionPickUpItem failed."

functions.name: ActionPutDownItem
doc: Put down oItem on the ground.
No return value, but if an error occurs the log file will contain
"ActionPutDownItem failed."

functions.name: GetLastAttacker
doc: Get the last attacker of oAttackee.  This should only be used ONLY in the
OnAttacked events for creatures, placeables and doors.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: ActionAttack
functions.signature: void ActionAttack(object oAttackee, int bPassive = FALSE)
doc: Attack oAttackee.
- bPassive: If this is TRUE, attack is in passive mode.

functions.name: GetNearestCreature
functions.params: int nFirstCriteriaType, int nFirstCriteriaValue, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1, int nSecondCriteriaType = -1, int nSecondCriteriaValue = -1, int nThirdCriteriaType = -1, int nThirdCriteriaValue = -1
functions.signature: object GetNearestCreature(int nFirstCriteriaType, int nFirstCriteriaValue, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1, int nSecondCriteriaType = -1, int nSecondCriteriaValue = -1, int nThirdCriteriaType = -1, int nThirdCriteriaValue = -1)
doc: Get the creature nearest to oTarget, subject to all the criteria specified.
- nFirstCriteriaType: CREATURE_TYPE_*
- nFirstCriteriaValue:
-> CLASS_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_CLASS
-> SPELL_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_DOES_NOT_HAVE_SPELL_EFFECT
or CREATURE_TYPE_HAS_SPELL_EFFECT
-> TRUE or FALSE if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_IS_ALIVE
-> PERCEPTION_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_PERCEPTION
-> PLAYER_CHAR_IS_PC or PLAYER_CHAR_NOT_PC if nFirstCriteriaType was
CREATURE_TYPE_PLAYER_CHAR
-> RACIAL_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_RACIAL_TYPE
-> REPUTATION_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_REPUTATION
For example, to get the nearest PC, use:
(CREATURE_TYPE_PLAYER_CHAR, PLAYER_CHAR_IS_PC)
- oTarget: We're trying to find the creature of the specified type that is
nearest to oTarget
- nNth: We don't have to find the first nearest: we can find the Nth nearest...
- nSecondCriteriaType: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaType to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nSecondCriteriaValue: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaValue
to further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nThirdCriteriaType: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaType to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nThirdCriteriaValue: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaValue to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: ActionSpeakString
functions.signature: void ActionSpeakString(string sStringToSpeak, int nTalkVolume = TALKVOLUME_TALK)
doc: Add a speak action to the action subject.
- sStringToSpeak: String to be spoken
- nTalkVolume: TALKVOLUME_*

functions.name: ActionPlayAnimation
functions.params: int nAnimation, float fSpeed = 1.0, float fDurationSeconds = 0.0
functions.signature: void ActionPlayAnimation(int nAnimation, float fSpeed = 1.0, float fDurationSeconds = 0.0)
doc: Cause the action subject to play an animation
- nAnimation: ANIMATION_*
- fSpeed: Speed of the animation
- fDurationSeconds: Duration of the animation (this is not used for Fire and
Forget animations)

functions.name: GetDistanceToObject
functions.signature: float GetDistanceToObject(object oObject, object oFrom = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Get the distance from oFrom to oObject in metres.
Return value on error: -1.0f

functions.name: GetIsObjectValid
doc: Returns TRUE if oObject is a valid object.

functions.name: ActionOpenDoor
functions.signature: void ActionOpenDoor(object oDoor, int bRun = FALSE)
doc: Cause the action subject to open oDoor
- bRun: If TRUE, subject will run to the door instead of walking

functions.name: ActionCloseDoor
functions.signature: void ActionCloseDoor(object oDoor, int bRun = FALSE)
doc: Cause the action subject to close oDoor
- bRun: If TRUE, subject will run to the door instead of walking

functions.name: SetCameraFacing
functions.params: float fDirection, float fDistance = -1.0f, float fPitch = -1.0, int nTransitionType = CAMERA_TRANSITION_TYPE_SNAP
functions.signature: void SetCameraFacing(float fDirection, float fDistance = -1.0f, float fPitch = -1.0, int nTransitionType = CAMERA_TRANSITION_TYPE_SNAP)
doc: Change the direction in which the camera is facing
- fDirection is expressed as anticlockwise degrees from Due East.
(0.0f=East, 90.0f=North, 180.0f=West, 270.0f=South)
A value of -1.0f for any parameter will be ignored and instead it will
use the current camera value.
This can be used to change the way the camera is facing after the player
emerges from an area transition.
- nTransitionType: CAMERA_TRANSITION_TYPE_*  SNAP will immediately move the
camera to the new position, while the other types will result in the camera moving gradually into position
Pitch and distance are limited to valid values for the current camera mode:
Top Down: Distance = 5-20, Pitch = 1-50
Driving camera: Distance = 6 (can't be changed), Pitch = 1-62
Chase: Distance = 5-20, Pitch = 1-50
NOTE *** In NWN:Hordes of the Underdark the camera limits have been relaxed to the following:
Distance 1-25
Pitch 1-89

functions.name: PlaySound
doc: Play sSoundName
- sSoundName: TBD - SS
This will play a mono sound from the location of the object running the command.

functions.name: GetSpellTargetObject
doc: Get the object at which the caller last cast a spell
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: ActionCastSpellAtObject
functions.params: int nSpell, object oTarget, int nMetaMagic = METAMAGIC_ANY, int bCheat = FALSE, int nDomainLevel = 0, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT, int bInstantSpell = FALSE, int nClass = -1, int bSpontaneousCast = FALSE
functions.signature: void ActionCastSpellAtObject(int nSpell, object oTarget, int nMetaMagic = METAMAGIC_ANY, int bCheat = FALSE, int nDomainLevel = 0, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT, int bInstantSpell = FALSE, int nClass = -1, int bSpontaneousCast = FALSE)
doc: This action casts a spell at oTarget.
- nSpell: SPELL_*
- oTarget: Target for the spell
- nMetaMagic: METAMAGIC_*. If nClass is specified, cannot be METAMAGIC_ANY.
- bCheat: If this is TRUE, then the executor of the action doesn't have to be
able to cast the spell. Ignored if nClass is specified.
- bCheat: If this is TRUE, then the executor of the action doesn't have to be
able to cast the spell.
- nDomainLevel: The level of the spell if cast from a domain slot.
eg SPELL_HEAL can be spell level 5 on a cleric. Use 0 for no domain slot.
- nProjectilePathType: PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_*
- bInstantSpell: If this is TRUE, the spell is cast immediately. This allows
the end-user to simulate a high-level magic-user having lots of advance
warning of impending trouble
- nClass: If set to a CLASS_TYPE_* it will cast using that class specifically.
CLASS_TYPE_INVALID will use spell abilities.
- bSpontaneousCast: If set to TRUE will attempt to cast the given spell spontaneously,
ie a Cleric casting Cure Light Wounds using any level 1 slot. Needs a valid nClass set.

functions.name: GetCurrentHitPoints
doc: Get the current hitpoints of oObject
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: GetMaxHitPoints
doc: Get the maximum hitpoints of oObject
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: GetLocalInt
doc: Get oObject's local integer variable sVarName
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: GetLocalFloat
doc: Get oObject's local float variable sVarName
Return value on error: 0.0f

functions.name: GetLocalString
doc: Get oObject's local string variable sVarName
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetLocalObject
doc: Get oObject's local object variable sVarName
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: SetLocalInt
functions.params: object oObject, string sVarName, int nValue
functions.signature: void SetLocalInt(object oObject, string sVarName, int nValue)

functions.name: SetLocalFloat
functions.params: object oObject, string sVarName, float fValue
functions.signature: void SetLocalFloat(object oObject, string sVarName, float fValue)
doc: Set oObject's local float variable sVarName to nValue

functions.name: SetLocalString
functions.params: object oObject, string sVarName, string sValue
functions.signature: void SetLocalString(object oObject, string sVarName, string sValue)
doc: Set oObject's local string variable sVarName to nValue

functions.name: SetLocalObject
functions.params: object oObject, string sVarName, object oValue
functions.signature: void SetLocalObject(object oObject, string sVarName, object oValue)
doc: Set oObject's local object variable sVarName to nValue

functions.name: GetStringLength
doc: Get the length of sString
Return value on error: -1

functions.name: GetStringUpperCase
doc: Convert sString into upper case
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetStringLowerCase
doc: Convert sString into lower case
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetStringRight
doc: Get nCount characters from the right end of sString
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetStringLeft
doc: Get nCounter characters from the left end of sString
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: InsertString
functions.params: string sDestination, string sString, int nPosition
functions.signature: string InsertString(string sDestination, string sString, int nPosition)
doc: Insert sString into sDestination at nPosition
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetSubString
functions.params: string sString, int nStart, int nCount
functions.signature: string GetSubString(string sString, int nStart, int nCount)
doc: Get nCount characters from sString, starting at nStart
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: FindSubString
functions.params: string sString, string sSubString, int nStart = 0
functions.signature: int FindSubString(string sString, string sSubString, int nStart = 0)
doc: Find the position of sSubstring inside sString
- nStart: The character position to start searching at (from the left end of the string).
Return value on error: -1

functions.name: fabs
doc: Maths operation: absolute value of fValue

functions.name: cos
doc: Maths operation: cosine of fValue

functions.name: sin
doc: Maths operation: sine of fValue

functions.name: tan
doc: Maths operation: tan of fValue

functions.name: acos
doc: Maths operation: arccosine of fValue
Returns zero if fValue > 1 or fValue < -1

functions.name: asin
doc: Maths operation: arcsine of fValue
Returns zero if fValue >1 or fValue < -1

functions.name: atan
doc: Maths operation: arctan of fValue

functions.name: log
doc: Maths operation: log of fValue
Returns zero if fValue <= zero

functions.name: pow
doc: Maths operation: fValue is raised to the power of fExponent
Returns zero if fValue ==0 and fExponent <0

functions.name: sqrt
doc: Maths operation: square root of fValue
Returns zero if fValue <0

functions.name: abs
doc: Maths operation: integer absolute value of nValue
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: EffectHeal
doc: Create a Heal effect. This should be applied as an instantaneous effect.
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nDamageToHeal < 0.

functions.name: EffectDamage
functions.params: int nDamageAmount, int nDamageType = DAMAGE_TYPE_MAGICAL, int nDamagePower = DAMAGE_POWER_NORMAL
functions.signature: effect EffectDamage(int nDamageAmount, int nDamageType = DAMAGE_TYPE_MAGICAL, int nDamagePower = DAMAGE_POWER_NORMAL)
doc: Create a Damage effect
- nDamageAmount: amount of damage to be dealt. This should be applied as an
instantaneous effect.
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*
- nDamagePower: DAMAGE_POWER_*

functions.name: EffectAbilityIncrease
doc: Create an Ability Increase effect
- bAbilityToIncrease: ABILITY_*

functions.name: EffectDamageResistance
functions.params: int nDamageType, int nAmount, int nLimit = 0, int bRangedOnly = FALSE
functions.signature: effect EffectDamageResistance(int nDamageType, int nAmount, int nLimit = 0, int bRangedOnly = FALSE)
doc: Create a Damage Resistance effect that removes the first nAmount points of
damage of type nDamageType, up to nLimit (or infinite if nLimit is 0)
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*
- nAmount: The amount of damage to soak each time the target is damaged.
- nLimit: How much damage the effect can absorb before disappearing.
Set to zero for infinite.
- bRangedOnly: Set to TRUE to have this resistance only apply to ranged attacks.

functions.name: EffectResurrection
doc: Create a Resurrection effect. This should be applied as an instantaneous effect.

functions.name: EffectSummonCreature
functions.params: string sCreatureResref, int nVisualEffectId = VFX_NONE, float fDelaySeconds = 0.0f, int nUseAppearAnimation = 0, int nUnsummonVisualEffectId = VFX_IMP_UNSUMMON, object oSummonToAdd = OBJECT_INVALID
functions.signature: effect EffectSummonCreature(string sCreatureResref, int nVisualEffectId = VFX_NONE, float fDelaySeconds = 0.0f, int nUseAppearAnimation = 0, int nUnsummonVisualEffectId = VFX_IMP_UNSUMMON, object oSummonToAdd = OBJECT_INVALID)
doc: Create a Summon Creature effect.  The creature is created and placed into the
caller's party/faction.
- sCreatureResref: Identifies the creature to be summoned
- nVisualEffectId: VFX_*
- fDelaySeconds: There can be delay between the visual effect being played, and the
creature being added to the area
- nUseAppearAnimation: should this creature play it's "appear" animation when it is
summoned. If zero, it will just fade in somewhere near the target.  If the value is 1
it will use the appear animation, and if it's 2 it will use appear2 (which doesn't exist for most creatures)
- nUnsummonVisualEffectId: VFX_* to apply when the creature is unsummoned
- oSummonToAdd: If sCreatureResref is blank, this object (if they have no master) is instead added as the summon, applying nVisualEffectId at their location
fDelaySeconds and nUseAppearAnimation are unused, and no "Summoned a creature" feedback is sent, allowing you to do your own.
The creature otherwise acts like a summon from then on, including not giving out XP for being killed, and able to be
unsummoned by the master or when the effect expires.

functions.name: GetCasterLevel
doc: Get the caster level of an object. This is consistent with the caster level used when applying effects if OBJECT_SELF is used.
- oObject: A creature will return the caster level of their currently cast spell or ability, or the item's caster level if an item was used
A placeable will return an automatic caster level: floor(10, (spell innate level * 2) - 1)
An Area of Effect object will return the caster level that was used to create the Area of Effect.
Return value on error, or if oObject has not yet cast a spell: 0;

functions.name: GetFirstEffect
doc: Get the first in-game effect on oCreature.

functions.name: GetNextEffect
doc: Get the next in-game effect on oCreature.

functions.name: RemoveEffect
doc: Remove eEffect from oCreature.
No return value

functions.name: GetIsEffectValid
doc: Returns TRUE if eEffect is a valid effect. The effect must have been applied to
an object or else it will return FALSE

functions.name: GetEffectDurationType
doc: Get the duration type (DURATION_TYPE_*) of eEffect.
Return value if eEffect is not valid: -1

functions.name: GetEffectSubType
doc: Get the subtype (SUBTYPE_*) of eEffect.
Return value on error: 0

functions.name: GetEffectCreator
doc: Get the object that created eEffect.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if eEffect is not a valid effect.

functions.name: IntToString
doc: Convert nInteger into a string.
Return value on error: ""

functions.name: GetFirstObjectInArea
functions.params: object oArea = OBJECT_INVALID, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_ALL
functions.signature: object GetFirstObjectInArea(object oArea = OBJECT_INVALID, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_ALL)
doc: Get the first object in oArea.
If no valid area is specified, it will use the caller's area.
- nObjectFilter: This allows you to filter out undesired object types, using bitwise "or".
For example, to return only creatures and doors, the value for this parameter would be OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE | OBJECT_TYPE_DOOR
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: GetNextObjectInArea
functions.params: object oArea = OBJECT_INVALID, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_ALL
functions.signature: object GetNextObjectInArea(object oArea = OBJECT_INVALID, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_ALL)
doc: Get the next object in oArea.
If no valid area is specified, it will use the caller's area.
- nObjectFilter: This allows you to filter out undesired object types, using bitwise "or".
For example, to return only creatures and doors, the value for this parameter would be OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE | OBJECT_TYPE_DOOR
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: d2
doc: Get the total from rolling (nNumDice x d2 dice).
- nNumDice: If this is less than 1, the value 1 will be used.

functions.name: d3
doc: Get the total from rolling (nNumDice x d3 dice).
- nNumDice: If this is less than 1, the value 1 will be used.

functions.name: d4

functions.name: GetFirstObjectInShape
functions.params: int nShape, float fSize, location lTarget, int bLineOfSight = FALSE, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE, vector vOrigin = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
functions.signature: object GetFirstObjectInShape(int nShape, float fSize, location lTarget, int bLineOfSight = FALSE, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE, vector vOrigin = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
doc: Get the first object in nShape
- nShape: SHAPE_*
- fSize:
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPHERE, this is the radius of the sphere
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPELLCYLINDER, this is the length of the cylinder
Spell Cylinder's always have a radius of 1.5m.
-> If nShape == SHAPE_CONE, this is the widest radius of the cone
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPELLCONE, this is the length of the cone in the
direction of lTarget. Spell cones are always 60 degrees with the origin
at OBJECT_SELF.
-> If nShape == SHAPE_CUBE, this is half the length of one of the sides of
the cube
- lTarget: This is the centre of the effect, usually GetSpellTargetLocation(),
or the end of a cylinder or cone.
- bLineOfSight: This controls whether to do a line-of-sight check on the
object returned. Line of sight check is done from origin to target object
at a height 1m above the ground
(This can be used to ensure that spell effects do not go through walls.)
- nObjectFilter: This allows you to filter out undesired object types, using
bitwise "or".
For example, to return only creatures and doors, the value for this
parameter would be OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE | OBJECT_TYPE_DOOR
- vOrigin: This is only used for cylinders and cones, and specifies the
origin of the effect(normally the spell-caster's position).
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: GetNextObjectInShape
functions.params: int nShape, float fSize, location lTarget, int bLineOfSight = FALSE, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE, vector vOrigin = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
functions.signature: object GetNextObjectInShape(int nShape, float fSize, location lTarget, int bLineOfSight = FALSE, int nObjectFilter = OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE, vector vOrigin = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
doc: Get the next object in nShape
- nShape: SHAPE_*
- fSize:
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPHERE, this is the radius of the sphere
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPELLCYLINDER, this is the length of the cylinder.
Spell Cylinder's always have a radius of 1.5m.
-> If nShape == SHAPE_CONE, this is the widest radius of the cone
-> If nShape == SHAPE_SPELLCONE, this is the length of the cone in the
direction of lTarget. Spell cones are always 60 degrees with the origin
at OBJECT_SELF.
-> If nShape == SHAPE_CUBE, this is half the length of one of the sides of
the cube
- lTarget: This is the centre of the effect, usually GetSpellTargetLocation(),
or the end of a cylinder or cone.
- bLineOfSight: This controls whether to do a line-of-sight check on the
object returned. (This can be used to ensure that spell effects do not go
through walls.) Line of sight check is done from origin to target object
at a height 1m above the ground
- nObjectFilter: This allows you to filter out undesired object types, using
bitwise "or". For example, to return only creatures and doors, the value for
this parameter would be OBJECT_TYPE_CREATURE | OBJECT_TYPE_DOOR
- vOrigin: This is only used for cylinders and cones, and specifies the origin
of the effect (normally the spell-caster's position).
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: EffectEntangle
doc: Create an Entangle effect
When applied, this effect will restrict the creature's movement and apply a
(-2) to all attacks and a -4 to AC.

functions.name: SignalEvent
doc: Causes object oObject to run the event evToRun. The script on the object that is
associated with the event specified will run.
Events can be created using the following event functions:
EventActivateItem() - This creates an OnActivateItem module event. The script for handling
this event can be set in Module Properties on the Event Tab.
EventConversation() - This creates on OnConversation creature event. The script for handling
this event can be set by viewing the Creature Properties on a
creature and then clicking on the Scripts Tab.
EventSpellCastAt()  - This creates an OnSpellCastAt event. The script for handling this
event can be set in the Scripts Tab of the Properties menu
for the object.
EventUserDefined()  - This creates on OnUserDefined event. The script for handling this event
can be set in the Scripts Tab of the Properties menu for the object/area/module.

functions.name: EventUserDefined
doc: Create an event of the type nUserDefinedEventNumber
Note: This only creates the event. The event wont actually trigger until SignalEvent()
is called using this created UserDefined event as an argument.
For example:
SignalEvent(oObject, EventUserDefined(9999));
Once the event has been signaled. The script associated with the OnUserDefined event will
run on the object oObject.
To specify the OnUserDefined script that should run, view the object's Properties
and click on the Scripts Tab. Then specify a script for the OnUserDefined event.
From inside the OnUserDefined script call:
GetUserDefinedEventNumber() to retrieve the value of nUserDefinedEventNumber
that was used when the event was signaled.

functions.name: EffectKnockdown
doc: Create a Knockdown effect

duration minimum (1 second to fall, 1 second sitting, 1 second to get up).

functions.name: ActionSit
doc: Sit in oChair.
Note: Not all creatures will be able to sit and not all
objects can be sat on.
The object oChair must also be marked as usable in the toolset.
For Example: To get a player to sit in oChair when they click on it,
place the following script in the OnUsed event for the object oChair.
void main()
object oChair = OBJECT_SELF;
AssignCommand(GetLastUsedBy(),ActionSit(oChair));

functions.name: GetTransitionTarget
doc: Get the destination object for the given object.
All objects can hold a transition target, but only Doors and Triggers
will be made clickable by the game engine (This may change in the
future). You can set and query transition targets on other objects for
your own scripted purposes.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oTransition does not hold a target.

functions.name: EffectLinkEffects
doc: Link the two supplied effects, returning eChildEffect as a child of
eParentEffect.
Note: When applying linked effects if the target is immune to all valid
effects all other effects will be removed as well. This means that if you
apply a visual effect and a silence effect (in a link) and the target is
immune to the silence effect that the visual effect will get removed as well.
Visual Effects are not considered "valid" effects for the purposes of
determining if an effect will be removed or not and as such should never be
packaged *only* with other visual effects in a link.

functions.name: GetObjectByTag
doc: Get the nNth object with the specified tag.
- sTag
- nNth: the nth object with this tag may be requested
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the object cannot be found.
Note: The module cannot be retrieved by GetObjectByTag(), use GetModule() instead.

functions.name: AdjustAlignment
functions.params: object oSubject, int nAlignment, int nShift, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE
functions.signature: void AdjustAlignment(object oSubject, int nAlignment, int nShift, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE)
doc: Adjust the alignment of oSubject.
- oSubject
- nAlignment:
-> ALIGNMENT_LAWFUL/ALIGNMENT_CHAOTIC/ALIGNMENT_GOOD/ALIGNMENT_EVIL: oSubject's
alignment will be shifted in the direction specified
-> ALIGNMENT_ALL: nShift will be added to oSubject's law/chaos and
good/evil alignment values
-> ALIGNMENT_NEUTRAL: nShift is applied to oSubject's law/chaos and
good/evil alignment values in the direction which is towards neutrality.
e.g. If oSubject has a law/chaos value of 10 (i.e. chaotic) and a
good/evil value of 80 (i.e. good) then if nShift is 15, the
law/chaos value will become (10+15)=25 and the good/evil value will
Furthermore, the shift will at most take the alignment value to 50 and
not beyond.
e.g. If oSubject has a law/chaos value of 40 and a good/evil value of 70,
then if nShift is 15, the law/chaos value will become 50 and the
good/evil value will become 55
- nShift: this is the desired shift in alignment
- bAllPartyMembers: when TRUE the alignment shift of oSubject also has a
diminished affect all members of oSubject's party (if oSubject is a Player).
When FALSE the shift only affects oSubject.
No return value

functions.name: ActionWait
doc: Do nothing for fSeconds seconds.

functions.name: SetAreaTransitionBMP
doc: Set the transition bitmap of a player; this should only be called in area

area transition via AssignCommand.
- nPredefinedAreaTransition:
-> To use a predefined area transition bitmap, use one of AREA_TRANSITION_*
-> To use a custom, user-defined area transition bitmap, use
AREA_TRANSITION_USER_DEFINED and specify the filename in the second
parameter
- sCustomAreaTransitionBMP: this is the filename of a custom, user-defined
area transition bitmap

functions.signature: effect EffectBeam(int nBeamVisualEffect, object oEffector, int nBodyPart, int bMissEffect = FALSE, float fScale = 1.0f, vector vTranslate = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0], vector vRotate = [0.0, 0.0, 0.0])

functions.name: GetReputation
doc: Get an integer between 0 and 100 (inclusive) that represents how oSource
feels about oTarget.
-> 0-10 means oSource is hostile to oTarget
-> 11-89 means oSource is neutral to oTarget
-> 90-100 means oSource is friendly to oTarget
Returns -1 if oSource or oTarget does not identify a valid object

functions.name: AdjustReputation
functions.params: object oTarget, object oSourceFactionMember, int nAdjustment
functions.signature: void AdjustReputation(object oTarget, object oSourceFactionMember, int nAdjustment)
doc: Adjust how oSourceFactionMember's faction feels about oTarget by the
specified amount.
Note: This adjusts Faction Reputation, how the entire faction that
oSourceFactionMember is in, feels about oTarget.
No return value
Note: You can't adjust a player character's (PC) faction towards
NPCs, so attempting to make an NPC hostile by passing in a PC object
as oSourceFactionMember in the following call will fail:
AdjustReputation(oNPC,oPC,-100);
Instead you should pass in the PC object as the first
parameter as in the following call which should succeed:
AdjustReputation(oPC,oNPC,-100);
Note: Will fail if oSourceFactionMember is a plot object.

functions.name: GetNearestCreatureToLocation
functions.params: int nFirstCriteriaType, int nFirstCriteriaValue, location lLocation, int nNth = 1, int nSecondCriteriaType = -1, int nSecondCriteriaValue = -1, int nThirdCriteriaType = -1, int nThirdCriteriaValue = -1
functions.signature: object GetNearestCreatureToLocation(int nFirstCriteriaType, int nFirstCriteriaValue, location lLocation, int nNth = 1, int nSecondCriteriaType = -1, int nSecondCriteriaValue = -1, int nThirdCriteriaType = -1, int nThirdCriteriaValue = -1)
doc: Get the creature nearest to lLocation, subject to all the criteria specified.
- nFirstCriteriaType: CREATURE_TYPE_*
- nFirstCriteriaValue:
-> CLASS_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_CLASS
-> SPELL_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_DOES_NOT_HAVE_SPELL_EFFECT
or CREATURE_TYPE_HAS_SPELL_EFFECT
-> TRUE or FALSE if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_IS_ALIVE
-> PERCEPTION_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_PERCEPTION
-> PLAYER_CHAR_IS_PC or PLAYER_CHAR_NOT_PC if nFirstCriteriaType was
CREATURE_TYPE_PLAYER_CHAR
-> RACIAL_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_RACIAL_TYPE
-> REPUTATION_TYPE_* if nFirstCriteriaType was CREATURE_TYPE_REPUTATION
For example, to get the nearest PC, use
(CREATURE_TYPE_PLAYER_CHAR, PLAYER_CHAR_IS_PC)
- lLocation: We're trying to find the creature of the specified type that is
nearest to lLocation
- nNth: We don't have to find the first nearest: we can find the Nth nearest....
- nSecondCriteriaType: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaType to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nSecondCriteriaValue: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaValue
to further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nThirdCriteriaType: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaType to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
- nThirdCriteriaValue: This is used in the same way as nFirstCriteriaValue to
further specify the type of creature that we are looking for.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: ActionCastSpellAtLocation
functions.params: int nSpell, location lTargetLocation, int nMetaMagic = METAMAGIC_ANY, int bCheat = FALSE, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT, int bInstantSpell = FALSE, int nClass = -1, int bSpontaneousCast = FALSE, int nDomainlevel = 0
functions.signature: void ActionCastSpellAtLocation(int nSpell, location lTargetLocation, int nMetaMagic = METAMAGIC_ANY, int bCheat = FALSE, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT, int bInstantSpell = FALSE, int nClass = -1, int bSpontaneousCast = FALSE, int nDomainlevel = 0)
doc: Cast spell nSpell at lTargetLocation.
- nSpell: SPELL_*
- lTargetLocation
- nMetaMagic: METAMAGIC_*. If nClass is specified, cannot be METAMAGIC_ANY.
- bCheat: If this is TRUE, then the executor of the action doesn't have to be
able to cast the spell. Ignored if nClass is specified.
- bCheat: If this is TRUE, then the executor of the action doesn't have to be
able to cast the spell.
- nProjectilePathType: PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_*
- bInstantSpell: If this is TRUE, the spell is cast immediately; this allows
the end-user to simulate
a high-level magic user having lots of advance warning of impending trouble.
- nClass: If set to a CLASS_TYPE_* it will cast using that class specifically.
CLASS_TYPE_INVALID will use spell abilities.
- bSpontaneousCast: If set to TRUE will attempt to cast the given spell spontaneously,
ie a Cleric casting Cure Light Wounds using any level 1 slot. Needs a valid nClass set.
- nDomainLevel: The level of the spell if cast from a domain slot.
eg SPELL_HEAL can be spell level 5 on a cleric. Use 0 for no domain slot.

functions.name: EventSpellCastAt
functions.params: object oCaster, int nSpell, int bHarmful = TRUE
functions.signature: event EventSpellCastAt(object oCaster, int nSpell, int bHarmful = TRUE)
doc: Create an event which triggers the "SpellCastAt" script
Note: This only creates the event. The event wont actually trigger until SignalEvent()
is called using this created SpellCastAt event as an argument.
For example:
SignalEvent(oCreature, EventSpellCastAt(oCaster, SPELL_MAGIC_MISSILE, TRUE));
This function doesn't cast the spell specified, it only creates an event so that
when the event is signaled on an object, the object will use its OnSpellCastAt script
to react to the spell being cast.
To specify the OnSpellCastAt script that should run, view the Object's Properties
and click on the Scripts Tab. Then specify a script for the OnSpellCastAt event.
From inside the OnSpellCastAt script call:
GetLastSpellCaster() to get the object that cast the spell (oCaster).
GetLastSpell() to get the type of spell cast (nSpell)
GetLastSpellHarmful() to determine if the spell cast at the object was harmful.

functions.name: GetLastSpellCaster

If used in an Area of Effect script it will return the ID of the spell that generated the AOE effect.
Returns the spell ID (SPELL_*) or -1 if no spell was cast or on error

functions.name: EventConversation
doc: Creates a conversation event.
Note: This only creates the event. The event wont actually trigger until SignalEvent()
is called using this created conversation event as an argument.
For example:
SignalEvent(oCreature, EventConversation());
Once the event has been signaled. The script associated with the OnConversation event will
run on the creature oCreature.
To specify the OnConversation script that should run, view the Creature Properties on
the creature and click on the Scripts Tab. Then specify a script for the OnConversation event.

functions.name: SetEncounterDifficulty
functions.signature: void SetEncounterDifficulty(int nEncounterDifficulty, object oEncounter = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Set the difficulty level of oEncounter.

- oEncounter

functions.name: GetEncounterDifficulty

functions.name: GetDistanceBetweenLocations

functions.name: GetReflexAdjustedDamage

functions.signature: int GetReflexAdjustedDamage(int nDamage, object oTarget, int nDC, int nSaveType = SAVING_THROW_TYPE_NONE, object oSaveVersus = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Use this in spell scripts to get nDamage adjusted by oTarget's reflex and
evasion saves.
- nDamage
- oTarget
- nDC: Difficulty check
- nSaveType: SAVING_THROW_TYPE_*
- oSaveVersus

functions.name: PlayAnimation
functions.params: int nAnimation, float fSpeed = 1.0, float fSeconds = 0.0
functions.signature: void PlayAnimation(int nAnimation, float fSpeed = 1.0, float fSeconds = 0.0)
doc: Play nAnimation immediately.
- nAnimation: ANIMATION_*
- fSpeed
- fSeconds

functions.name: TalentSpell
doc: Create a Spell Talent.
- nSpell: SPELL_*

functions.name: TalentFeat
doc: Create a Feat Talent.
- nFeat: FEAT_*

functions.name: TalentSkill
doc: Create a Skill Talent.
- nSkill: SKILL_*

functions.name: GetHasSpellEffect
functions.signature: int GetHasSpellEffect(int nSpell, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determines whether oObject has any effects applied by nSpell
- nSpell: SPELL_*
- oObject
The spell id on effects is only valid if the effect is created
when the spell script runs. If it is created in a delayed command
then the spell id on the effect will be invalid.

functions.name: GetEffectSpellId
doc: Get the spell (SPELL_*) that applied eSpellEffect.
Returns -1 if eSpellEffect was applied outside a spell script.

functions.name: GetCreatureHasTalent
functions.signature: int GetCreatureHasTalent(talent tTalent, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determine whether oCreature has tTalent.

functions.name: GetCreatureTalentRandom
functions.signature: talent GetCreatureTalentRandom(int nCategory, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Get a random talent of oCreature, within nCategory.
- nCategory: TALENT_CATEGORY_*
- oCreature

functions.name: GetCreatureTalentBest
functions.params: int nCategory, int nCRMax, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF
functions.signature: talent GetCreatureTalentBest(int nCategory, int nCRMax, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Get the best talent (i.e. closest to nCRMax without going over) of oCreature,
within nCategory.
- nCategory: TALENT_CATEGORY_*
- nCRMax: Challenge Rating of the talent
- oCreature

functions.name: ActionUseTalentOnObject
doc: Use tChosenTalent on oTarget.

functions.name: ActionUseTalentAtLocation
doc: Use tChosenTalent at lTargetLocation.

functions.name: GetGoldPieceValue
doc: Get the gold piece value of oItem.
Returns 0 if oItem is not a valid item.

functions.name: GetIsPlayableRacialType
doc: Returns TRUE if oCreature is of a playable racial type.

functions.name: JumpToLocation
doc: Jump to lDestination.  The action is added to the TOP of the action queue.

functions.name: EffectTemporaryHitpoints
doc: Create a Temporary Hitpoints effect.
- nHitPoints: a positive integer
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nHitPoints < 0.

functions.name: GetSkillRank
functions.params: int nSkill, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nBaseSkillRank = FALSE
functions.signature: int GetSkillRank(int nSkill, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nBaseSkillRank = FALSE)
doc: Get the number of ranks that oTarget has in nSkill.
- nSkill: SKILL_*
- oTarget
- nBaseSkillRank: if set to true returns the number of base skill ranks the target
has (i.e. not including any bonuses from ability scores, feats, etc).
Returns -1 if oTarget doesn't have nSkill.
Returns 0 if nSkill is untrained.

functions.name: GetAttackTarget
doc: Get the attack target of oCreature.
This only works when oCreature is in combat.

functions.name: GetLastAttackType
doc: Get the attack type (SPECIAL_ATTACK_*) of oCreature's last attack.
This only works when oCreature is in combat.

functions.name: GetLastAttackMode
doc: Get the attack mode (COMBAT_MODE_*) of oCreature's last attack.
This only works when oCreature is in combat.

functions.name: GetMaster
doc: Get the master of oAssociate.

functions.name: GetIsInCombat
doc: Returns TRUE if oCreature is in combat.

functions.name: GetLastAssociateCommand
doc: Get the last command (ASSOCIATE_COMMAND_*) issued to oAssociate.

functions.name: GiveGoldToCreature
doc: Give nGP gold to oCreature.

functions.name: SetIsDestroyable
functions.params: int bDestroyable, int bRaiseable = TRUE, int bSelectableWhenDead = FALSE, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF
functions.signature: void SetIsDestroyable(int bDestroyable, int bRaiseable = TRUE, int bSelectableWhenDead = FALSE, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Set the destroyable status of oObject
- bDestroyable: If this is FALSE, the caller does not fade out on death, but
sticks around as a corpse.
- bRaiseable: If this is TRUE, the caller can be raised via resurrection.
- bSelectableWhenDead: If this is TRUE, the caller is selectable after death.
- oObject: Object to affect.

functions.name: SetLocked
doc: Set the locked state of oTarget, which can be a door or a placeable object.

functions.name: GetLocked
doc: Get the locked state of oTarget, which can be a door or a placeable object.

functions.name: GetClickingObject
doc: Use this in a trigger's OnClick event script to get the object that last
clicked on it.
This is identical to GetEnteringObject.
GetClickingObject() should not be called from a placeable's OnClick event,
instead use GetPlaceableLastClickedBy();

functions.name: SetAssociateListenPatterns
doc: Initialise oTarget to listen for the standard Associates commands.

functions.name: GetLastWeaponUsed
doc: Get the last weapon that oCreature used in an attack.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oCreature did not attack, or has no weapon equipped.

functions.name: ActionInteractObject
doc: Use oPlaceable.

functions.name: GetLastUsedBy
doc: Get the last object that used the placeable object that is calling this function.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if it is called by something other than a placeable or
a door.

functions.name: GetAbilityModifier
functions.signature: int GetAbilityModifier(int nAbility, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Returns the ability modifier for the specified ability
Get oCreature's ability modifier for nAbility.
- nAbility: ABILITY_*
- oCreature

functions.name: GetIdentified
doc: Determined whether oItem has been identified.

functions.name: SetIdentified
doc: Set whether oItem has been identified.

functions.name: SummonAnimalCompanion
doc: Summon an Animal Companion

functions.name: SummonFamiliar
doc: Summon a Familiar

functions.name: GetBlockingDoor
doc: Get the last blocking door encountered by the caller of this function.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the caller is not a valid creature.

functions.name: GetIsDoorActionPossible
doc: - oTargetDoor
- nDoorAction: DOOR_ACTION_*
Returns TRUE if nDoorAction can be performed on oTargetDoor.

functions.name: DoDoorAction
doc: Perform nDoorAction on oTargetDoor.

functions.name: GetFirstItemInInventory
doc: Get the first item in oTarget's inventory (start to cycle through oTarget's
inventory).
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oTarget is not a creature, item, placeable or store,
or if no item is found.

functions.name: GetNextItemInInventory
doc: Get the next item in oTarget's inventory (continue to cycle through oTarget's
inventory).
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oTarget is not a creature, item, placeable or store,
or if no item is found.

functions.name: GetClassByPosition
functions.signature: int GetClassByPosition(int nClassPosition, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: A creature can have up to three classes.  This function determines the
creature's class (CLASS_TYPE_*) based on nClassPosition.
- nClassPosition: 1, 2 or 3
- oCreature
Returns CLASS_TYPE_INVALID if the oCreature does not have a class in
nClassPosition (i.e. a single-class creature will only have a value in
nClassLocation=1) or if oCreature is not a valid creature.

functions.name: GetLevelByPosition
functions.signature: int GetLevelByPosition(int nClassPosition, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: A creature can have up to three classes.  This function determines the
creature's class level based on nClass Position.
- nClassPosition: 1, 2 or 3
- oCreature
Returns 0 if oCreature does not have a class in nClassPosition
(i.e. a single-class creature will only have a value in nClassLocation=1)
or if oCreature is not a valid creature.

functions.name: GetLevelByClass
functions.signature: int GetLevelByClass(int nClassType, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determine the levels that oCreature holds in nClassType.
- nClassType: CLASS_TYPE_*
- oCreature

functions.name: GetDamageDealtByType
doc: Get the amount of damage of type nDamageType that has been dealt to the caller.
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*

functions.name: GetTotalDamageDealt
doc: Get the total amount of damage that has been dealt to the caller.

functions.name: GetLastDamager
doc: Get the last object that damaged oObject
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oObject is not a valid object.

functions.name: GetLastDisarmed
doc: Get the last object that disarmed the trap on the caller.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the caller is not a valid placeable, trigger or
door.

functions.name: GetLastDisturbed
doc: Get the last object that disturbed the inventory of oObject.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oObject is not a valid creature or placeable.

functions.name: GetLastLocked
doc: Get the last object that locked oObject.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oObject is not a valid door or placeable.

functions.name: GetLastUnlocked
doc: Get the last object that unlocked oObject.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oObject is not a valid door or placeable.

functions.name: EffectSkillIncrease
doc: Create a Skill Increase effect.
- nSkill: SKILL_*
- nValue
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nSkill is invalid.

functions.name: GetInventoryDisturbType
doc: Get the type of disturbance (INVENTORY_DISTURB_*) that caused the oObject's
OnInventoryDisturbed script to fire.  This will only work for creatures and
placeables.

functions.name: GetInventoryDisturbItem
doc: get the item that caused oObject's OnInventoryDisturbed script to fire.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the oObject is not a valid object.

functions.name: GetHenchman
functions.signature: object GetHenchman(object oMaster = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1)
doc: Get the henchman belonging to oMaster.
Return OBJECT_INVALID if oMaster does not have a henchman.
-nNth: Which henchman to return.

functions.name: VersusAlignmentEffect
functions.params: effect eEffect, int nLawChaos = ALIGNMENT_ALL, int nGoodEvil = ALIGNMENT_ALL
functions.signature: effect VersusAlignmentEffect(effect eEffect, int nLawChaos = ALIGNMENT_ALL, int nGoodEvil = ALIGNMENT_ALL)
doc: Set eEffect to be versus a specific alignment.
- eEffect

- nGoodEvil: ALIGNMENT_GOOD/ALIGNMENT_EVIL/ALIGNMENT_ALL

doc: Set eEffect to be versus nRacialType.
- eEffect
- nRacialType: RACIAL_TYPE_*

functions.name: VersusTrapEffect
doc: Set eEffect to be versus traps.

functions.name: GetGender
doc: Get the gender of oCreature.

functions.name: GetIsTalentValid
doc: Returns TRUE if tTalent is valid.

functions.params: location lMoveAwayFrom, int bRun = FALSE, float fMoveAwayRange = 40.0f

doc: Causes the action subject to move away from lMoveAwayFrom.

functions.name: GetAttemptedAttackTarget
doc: Get the target that the caller attempted to attack - this should be used in
conjunction with GetAttackTarget(). This value is set every time an attack is
made, and is reset at the end of combat.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the caller is not a valid creature.

functions.name: GetTypeFromTalent
doc: Get the type (TALENT_TYPE_*) of tTalent.

functions.name: GetIdFromTalent
doc: Get the ID of tTalent.  This could be a SPELL_*, FEAT_* or SKILL_*.

functions.name: GetAssociate
functions.params: int nAssociateType, object oMaster = OBJECT_SELF, int nTh = 1
functions.signature: object GetAssociate(int nAssociateType, object oMaster = OBJECT_SELF, int nTh = 1)
doc: Get the associate of type nAssociateType belonging to oMaster.
- nAssociateType: ASSOCIATE_TYPE_*
- nMaster
- nTh: Which associate of the specified type to return
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if no such associate exists.

functions.name: AddHenchman
functions.signature: void AddHenchman(object oMaster, object oHenchman = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Add oHenchman as a henchman to oMaster
If oHenchman is either a DM or a player character, this will have no effect.

functions.name: RemoveHenchman
functions.signature: void RemoveHenchman(object oMaster, object oHenchman = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Remove oHenchman from the service of oMaster, returning them to their original faction.

functions.name: AddJournalQuestEntry
functions.params: string szPlotID, int nState, object oCreature, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE, int bAllPlayers = FALSE, int bAllowOverrideHigher = FALSE
functions.signature: void AddJournalQuestEntry(string szPlotID, int nState, object oCreature, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE, int bAllPlayers = FALSE, int bAllowOverrideHigher = FALSE)
doc: Add a journal quest entry to oCreature.
- szPlotID: the plot identifier used in the toolset's Journal Editor
- nState: the state of the plot as seen in the toolset's Journal Editor
- oCreature
- bAllPartyMembers: If this is TRUE, the entry will show up in the journal of
everyone in the party
- bAllPlayers: If this is TRUE, the entry will show up in the journal of
everyone in the world
- bAllowOverrideHigher: If this is TRUE, you can set the state to a lower
number than the one it is currently on

functions.name: RemoveJournalQuestEntry
functions.params: string szPlotID, object oCreature, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE, int bAllPlayers = FALSE
functions.signature: void RemoveJournalQuestEntry(string szPlotID, object oCreature, int bAllPartyMembers = TRUE, int bAllPlayers = FALSE)
doc: Remove a journal quest entry from oCreature.
- szPlotID: the plot identifier used in the toolset's Journal Editor
- oCreature
- bAllPartyMembers: If this is TRUE, the entry will be removed from the
journal of everyone in the party
- bAllPlayers: If this is TRUE, the entry will be removed from the journal of
everyone in the world

functions.name: GetPCPublicCDKey
functions.signature: string GetPCPublicCDKey(object oPlayer, int nSinglePlayerCDKey = FALSE)
doc: Get the public part of the CD Key that oPlayer used when logging in.
- nSinglePlayerCDKey: If set to TRUE, the player's public CD Key will
be returned when the player is playing in single player mode
(otherwise returns an empty string in single player mode).

functions.name: GetPCIPAddress
doc: Get the IP address from which oPlayer has connected.

functions.name: GetPCPlayerName
doc: Get the name of oPlayer.

functions.name: SetPCLike
doc: Sets oPlayer and oTarget to like each other.

functions.name: SetPCDislike
doc: Sets oPlayer and oTarget to dislike each other.

functions.name: SendMessageToPC
doc: Send a server message (szMessage) to the oPlayer.

functions.name: GetAttemptedSpellTarget
doc: Get the target at which the caller attempted to cast a spell.
This value is set every time a spell is cast and is reset at the end of
combat.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if the caller is not a valid creature.

functions.name: GetLastOpenedBy
doc: Get the last creature that opened oObject.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oObject is not a valid door, placeable or store.

functions.name: GetHasSpell
functions.signature: int GetHasSpell(int nSpell, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determines the number of times that oCreature has nSpell memorised.
- nSpell: SPELL_*
- oCreature

functions.name: OpenStore
functions.params: object oStore, object oPC, int nBonusMarkUp = 0, int nBonusMarkDown = 0
functions.signature: void OpenStore(object oStore, object oPC, int nBonusMarkUp = 0, int nBonusMarkDown = 0)
doc: Open oStore for oPC.
- nBonusMarkUp is added to the stores default mark up percentage on items sold (-100 to 100)
- nBonusMarkDown is added to the stores default mark down percentage on items bought (-100 to 100)

functions.name: EffectTurned
doc: Create a Turned effect.
Turned effects are supernatural by default.

functions.name: GetFirstFactionMember
functions.signature: object GetFirstFactionMember(object oMemberOfFaction, int bPCOnly = TRUE)
doc: Get the first member of oMemberOfFaction's faction (start to cycle through
oMemberOfFaction's faction).
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oMemberOfFaction's faction is invalid.

functions.name: GetNextFactionMember
functions.signature: object GetNextFactionMember(object oMemberOfFaction, int bPCOnly = TRUE)
doc: Get the next member of oMemberOfFaction's faction (continue to cycle through
oMemberOfFaction's faction).
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oMemberOfFaction's faction is invalid.

functions.name: ActionForceMoveToLocation
functions.params: location lDestination, int bRun = FALSE, float fTimeout = 30.0f
functions.signature: void ActionForceMoveToLocation(location lDestination, int bRun = FALSE, float fTimeout = 30.0f)
doc: Force the action subject to move to lDestination.

functions.name: ActionForceMoveToObject
functions.params: object oMoveTo, int bRun = FALSE, float fRange = 1.0f, float fTimeout = 30.0f
functions.signature: void ActionForceMoveToObject(object oMoveTo, int bRun = FALSE, float fRange = 1.0f, float fTimeout = 30.0f)
doc: Force the action subject to move to oMoveTo.

functions.name: GetJournalQuestExperience
doc: Get the experience assigned in the journal editor for szPlotID.

functions.name: JumpToObject
doc: Jump to oToJumpTo (the action is added to the top of the action queue).

functions.name: SetMapPinEnabled
doc: Set whether oMapPin is enabled.
- oMapPin
- nEnabled: 0=Off, 1=On

functions.name: EffectHitPointChangeWhenDying
doc: Create a Hit Point Change When Dying effect.
- fHitPointChangePerRound: this can be positive or negative, but not zero.
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if fHitPointChangePerRound is 0.

functions.name: PopUpGUIPanel
doc: Spawn a GUI panel for the client that controls oPC.
Will force show panels disabled with SetGuiPanelDisabled()
- oPC
- nGUIPanel: GUI_PANEL_*, except GUI_PANEL_COMPASS / GUI_PANEL_LEVELUP / GUI_PANEL_GOLD_* / GUI_PANEL_EXAMINE_*
Nothing happens if oPC is not a player character or if an invalid value is used for nGUIPanel.

functions.name: ClearPersonalReputation
functions.signature: void ClearPersonalReputation(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Clear all personal feelings that oSource has about oTarget.

functions.name: SetIsTemporaryFriend
functions.params: object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f
functions.signature: void SetIsTemporaryFriend(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f)
doc: oSource will temporarily be friends towards oTarget.
bDecays determines whether the personal reputation value decays over time
fDurationInSeconds is the length of time that the temporary friendship lasts
Make oSource into a temporary friend of oTarget using personal reputation.
- oTarget
- oSource
- bDecays: If this is TRUE, the friendship decays over fDurationInSeconds;
otherwise it is indefinite.
- fDurationInSeconds: This is only used if bDecays is TRUE, it is how long
the friendship lasts.
Note: If bDecays is TRUE, the personal reputation amount decreases in size
over fDurationInSeconds. Friendship will only be in effect as long as
(faction reputation + total personal reputation) >= REPUTATION_TYPE_FRIEND.

functions.name: SetIsTemporaryEnemy
functions.params: object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f
functions.signature: void SetIsTemporaryEnemy(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f)
doc: Make oSource into a temporary enemy of oTarget using personal reputation.
- oTarget
- oSource
- bDecays: If this is TRUE, the enmity decays over fDurationInSeconds;
otherwise it is indefinite.
- fDurationInSeconds: This is only used if bDecays is TRUE, it is how long
the enmity lasts.
Note: If bDecays is TRUE, the personal reputation amount decreases in size
over fDurationInSeconds. Enmity will only be in effect as long as
(faction reputation + total personal reputation) <= REPUTATION_TYPE_ENEMY.

functions.name: SetIsTemporaryNeutral
functions.params: object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f
functions.signature: void SetIsTemporaryNeutral(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int bDecays = FALSE, float fDurationInSeconds = 180.0f)
doc: Make oSource temporarily neutral to oTarget using personal reputation.
- oTarget
- oSource
- bDecays: If this is TRUE, the neutrality decays over fDurationInSeconds;
otherwise it is indefinite.
- fDurationInSeconds: This is only used if bDecays is TRUE, it is how long
the neutrality lasts.
Note: If bDecays is TRUE, the personal reputation amount decreases in size
over fDurationInSeconds. Neutrality will only be in effect as long as
(faction reputation + total personal reputation) > REPUTATION_TYPE_ENEMY and
(faction reputation + total personal reputation) < REPUTATION_TYPE_FRIEND.

functions.name: GiveXPToCreature
doc: Gives nXpAmount to oCreature.

functions.name: SetXP
doc: Sets oCreature's experience to nXpAmount.

functions.name: GetXP
doc: Get oCreature's experience.

functions.name: IntToHexString
doc: Convert nInteger to hex, returning the hex value as a string.
Return value has the format "0x????????" where each ? will be a hex digit
(8 digits in total).

functions.name: GetBaseItemType
doc: Get the base item type (BASE_ITEM_*) of oItem.
Returns BASE_ITEM_INVALID if oItem is an invalid item.

functions.name: GetItemHasItemProperty
doc: Determines whether oItem has nProperty.
- oItem
- nProperty: ITEM_PROPERTY_*
Returns FALSE if oItem is not a valid item, or if oItem does not have
nProperty.

functions.name: ActionEquipMostDamagingMelee
functions.params: object oVersus = OBJECT_INVALID, int bOffHand = FALSE
functions.signature: void ActionEquipMostDamagingMelee(object oVersus = OBJECT_INVALID, int bOffHand = FALSE)
doc: The creature will equip the melee weapon in its possession that can do the
most damage. If no valid melee weapon is found, it will equip the most
damaging range weapon. This function should only ever be called in the
EndOfCombatRound scripts, because otherwise it would have to stop the combat
round to run simulation.
- oVersus: You can try to get the most damaging weapon against oVersus
- bOffHand

functions.name: ActionEquipMostDamagingRanged
doc: The creature will equip the range weapon in its possession that can do the
most damage.
If no valid range weapon can be found, it will equip the most damaging melee
weapon.
- oVersus: You can try to get the most damaging weapon against oVersus

functions.name: GetItemACValue
doc: Get the Armour Class of oItem.
Return 0 if the oItem is not a valid item, or if oItem has no armour value.

functions.name: ActionRest
doc: The creature will rest if not in combat and no enemies are nearby.
- bCreatureToEnemyLineOfSightCheck: TRUE to allow the creature to rest if enemies
are nearby, but the creature can't see the enemy.
FALSE the creature will not rest if enemies are
nearby regardless of whether or not the creature
can see them, such as if an enemy is close by,
but is in a different room behind a closed door.

functions.name: ExploreAreaForPlayer
functions.params: object oArea, object oPlayer, int bExplored = TRUE
functions.signature: void ExploreAreaForPlayer(object oArea, object oPlayer, int bExplored = TRUE)
doc: Expose/Hide the entire map of oArea for oPlayer.
- oArea: The area that the map will be exposed/hidden for.
- oPlayer: The player the map will be exposed/hidden for.
- bExplored: TRUE/FALSE. Whether the map should be completely explored or hidden.

functions.name: ActionEquipMostEffectiveArmor
doc: The creature will equip the armour in its possession that has the highest
armour class.

functions.name: GetIsDay
doc: Returns TRUE if it is currently day.

functions.name: GetIsNight
doc: Returns TRUE if it is currently night.

functions.name: GetIsDawn
doc: Returns TRUE if it is currently dawn.

functions.name: GetIsDusk
doc: Returns TRUE if it is currently dusk.

functions.name: GetIsEncounterCreature
doc: Returns TRUE if oCreature was spawned from an encounter.

functions.name: GetLastPlayerDying
doc: Use this in an OnPlayerDying module script to get the last player who is dying.

functions.name: GetStartingLocation
doc: Get the starting location of the module.

functions.name: ChangeToStandardFaction
doc: Make oCreatureToChange join one of the standard factions.
This will only work on an NPC **
- nStandardFaction: STANDARD_FACTION_*

functions.name: SoundObjectPlay
doc: Play oSound.

functions.name: SoundObjectStop
doc: Stop playing oSound.

functions.name: SoundObjectSetVolume
doc: Set the volume of oSound.
- oSound
- nVolume: 0-127

functions.name: SoundObjectSetPosition
doc: Set the position of oSound.

functions.name: SpeakOneLinerConversation
functions.signature: void SpeakOneLinerConversation(string sDialogResRef = "", object oTokenTarget = OBJECT_TYPE_INVALID)
doc: Immediately speak a conversation one-liner.
- sDialogResRef
- oTokenTarget: This must be specified if there are creature-specific tokens
in the string.

functions.name: GetGold
doc: Get the amount of gold possessed by oTarget.

functions.name: GetLastRespawnButtonPresser
doc: Use this in an OnRespawnButtonPressed module script to get the object id of
the player who last pressed the respawn button.

functions.name: GetIsDM
doc: Returns TRUE if oCreature is the Dungeon Master.
Note: This will return FALSE if oCreature is a DM Possessed creature.
To determine if oCreature is a DM Possessed creature, use GetIsDMPossessed()

functions.name: PlayVoiceChat
functions.signature: void PlayVoiceChat(int nVoiceChatID, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Play a voice chat.
- nVoiceChatID: VOICE_CHAT_*
- oTarget

functions.name: GetIsWeaponEffective

functions.signature: int GetIsWeaponEffective(object oVersus = OBJECT_INVALID, int bOffHand = FALSE)

functions.name: GetLastSpellHarmful

harmful.
Returns TRUE if the last spell cast was harmful.

functions.name: EventActivateItem
functions.params: object oItem, location lTarget, object oTarget = OBJECT_INVALID

doc: Activate oItem.

functions.name: MusicBackgroundPlay

functions.name: MusicBackgroundStop

functions.name: MusicBackgroundSetDelay
doc: Set the delay for the background music for oArea.
