- nDelay: delay in milliseconds

functions.name: MusicBackgroundChangeDay
doc: Change the background day track for oArea to nTrack.
- oArea
- nTrack

functions.name: MusicBackgroundChangeNight
doc: Change the background night track for oArea to nTrack.
- oArea
- nTrack

functions.name: MusicBattlePlay
doc: Play the battle music for oArea.

functions.name: MusicBattleStop
doc: Stop the battle music for oArea.

functions.name: MusicBattleChange
doc: Change the battle track for oArea.
- oArea
- nTrack

functions.name: AmbientSoundPlay
doc: Play the ambient sound for oArea.

functions.name: AmbientSoundStop
doc: Stop the ambient sound for oArea.

functions.name: AmbientSoundChangeDay
doc: Change the ambient day track for oArea to nTrack.
- oArea
- nTrack

functions.name: AmbientSoundChangeNight
doc: Change the ambient night track for oArea to nTrack.
- oArea
- nTrack

functions.name: GetLastKiller
doc: Get the object that killed the caller.

functions.name: GetSpellCastItem
doc: Use this in a spell script to get the item used to cast the spell.

functions.name: GetItemActivated
doc: Use this in an OnItemActivated module script to get the item that was activated.

functions.name: GetItemActivator
doc: Use this in an OnItemActivated module script to get the creature that
activated the item.

functions.name: GetItemActivatedTargetLocation
doc: Use this in an OnItemActivated module script to get the location of the item's
target.

functions.name: GetItemActivatedTarget
doc: Use this in an OnItemActivated module script to get the item's target.

functions.name: GetIsOpen
doc: Returns TRUE if oObject (which is a placeable or a door) is currently open.

functions.name: TakeGoldFromCreature
functions.params: int nAmount, object oCreatureToTakeFrom, int bDestroy = FALSE
functions.signature: void TakeGoldFromCreature(int nAmount, object oCreatureToTakeFrom, int bDestroy = FALSE)
doc: Take nAmount of gold from oCreatureToTakeFrom.
- nAmount
- oCreatureToTakeFrom: If this is not a valid creature, nothing will happen.
- bDestroy: If this is TRUE, the caller will not get the gold.  Instead, the
gold will be destroyed and will vanish from the game.

functions.name: IsInConversation
doc: Determine whether oObject is in conversation.

functions.name: EffectAbilityDecrease
doc: Create an Ability Decrease effect.
- nAbility: ABILITY_*
- nModifyBy: This is the amount by which to decrement the ability

functions.name: EffectAttackDecrease
functions.signature: effect EffectAttackDecrease(int nPenalty, int nModifierType = ATTACK_BONUS_MISC)
doc: Create an Attack Decrease effect.
- nPenalty
- nModifierType: ATTACK_BONUS_*

functions.name: EffectDamageDecrease
functions.signature: effect EffectDamageDecrease(int nPenalty, int nDamageType = DAMAGE_TYPE_MAGICAL)
doc: Create a Damage Decrease effect.
- nPenalty
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*

functions.name: EffectDamageImmunityDecrease
doc: Create a Damage Immunity Decrease effect.
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*
- nPercentImmunity

functions.name: EffectACDecrease
functions.params: int nValue, int nModifyType = AC_DODGE_BONUS, int nDamageType = AC_VS_DAMAGE_TYPE_ALL
functions.signature: effect EffectACDecrease(int nValue, int nModifyType = AC_DODGE_BONUS, int nDamageType = AC_VS_DAMAGE_TYPE_ALL)
doc: Create an AC Decrease effect.
- nValue
- nModifyType: AC_*
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*
Default value for nDamageType should only ever be used in this function prototype.

functions.name: EffectMovementSpeedDecrease
doc: Create a Movement Speed Decrease effect.
- nPercentChange - range 0 through 99
eg.
0 = no change in speed
50 = 50% slower
99 = almost immobile

functions.name: EffectSavingThrowDecrease
functions.params: int nSave, int nValue, int nSaveType = SAVING_THROW_TYPE_ALL
functions.signature: effect EffectSavingThrowDecrease(int nSave, int nValue, int nSaveType = SAVING_THROW_TYPE_ALL)
doc: Create a Saving Throw Decrease effect.
- nSave: SAVING_THROW_* (not SAVING_THROW_TYPE_*)
SAVING_THROW_ALL
SAVING_THROW_FORT
SAVING_THROW_REFLEX
SAVING_THROW_WILL
- nValue: size of the Saving Throw decrease
- nSaveType: SAVING_THROW_TYPE_* (e.g. SAVING_THROW_TYPE_ACID )

functions.name: EffectSkillDecrease
doc: Create a Skill Decrease effect.
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nSkill is invalid.

functions.name: EffectSpellResistanceDecrease
doc: Create a Spell Resistance Decrease effect.

functions.name: GetPlotFlag
doc: Determine whether oTarget is a plot object.

functions.name: SetPlotFlag
doc: Set oTarget's plot object status.

functions.name: EffectInvisibility
doc: Create an Invisibility effect.
- nInvisibilityType: INVISIBILITY_TYPE_*
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nInvisibilityType
is invalid.

functions.name: EffectConcealment
functions.signature: effect EffectConcealment(int nPercentage, int nMissType = MISS_CHANCE_TYPE_NORMAL)
doc: Create a Concealment effect.
- nPercentage: 1-100 inclusive
- nMissChanceType: MISS_CHANCE_TYPE_*
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nPercentage < 1 or
nPercentage > 100.

functions.name: EffectDarkness
doc: Create a Darkness effect.

functions.name: EffectDispelMagicAll
doc: Create a Dispel Magic All effect.
If no parameter is specified, USE_CREATURE_LEVEL will be used. This will
cause the dispel effect to use the level of the creature that created the
effect.

functions.name: EffectUltravision
doc: Create an Ultravision effect.

functions.name: EffectNegativeLevel
functions.signature: effect EffectNegativeLevel(int nNumLevels, int bHPBonus = FALSE)
doc: Create a Negative Level effect.
- nNumLevels: the number of negative levels to apply.
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nNumLevels > 100.

functions.name: EffectPolymorph
functions.params: int nPolymorphSelection, int nLocked = FALSE, int nUnpolymorphVFX = VFX_IMP_POLYMORPH, int nSpellAbilityModifier = -1, int nSpellAbilityCasterLevel = 0
functions.signature: effect EffectPolymorph(int nPolymorphSelection, int nLocked = FALSE, int nUnpolymorphVFX = VFX_IMP_POLYMORPH, int nSpellAbilityModifier = -1, int nSpellAbilityCasterLevel = 0)
doc: Create a Polymorph effect.
- nLocked: If TRUE the creature cannot cancel the polymorph.
- nUnpolymorphVFX: If -1 no VFX will play when this polymorph is removed. Else will play the relevant VFX.
- nSpellAbilityModifier: Set a custom spell ability modifier for the 3 polymorph spells.
Save DC is 10 + Innate spell level + this ability modifier.
-1 uses the creators spellcasting/feat using class spellcasting ability modifier.
- nSpellAbilityCasterLevel: Set a custom caster level for the 3 polymorph spells.
Default (0) is to use the first class slot class level as previously.

functions.name: EffectSanctuary
doc: Create a Sanctuary effect.
- nDifficultyClass: must be a non-zero, positive number
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nDifficultyClass <= 0.

functions.name: EffectTrueSeeing
doc: Create a True Seeing effect.

functions.name: EffectSeeInvisible
doc: Create a See Invisible effect.

functions.name: EffectTimeStop
doc: Create a Time Stop effect.

functions.name: EffectBlindness
doc: Create a Blindness effect.

functions.name: GetIsReactionTypeFriendly
functions.signature: int GetIsReactionTypeFriendly(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determine whether oSource has a friendly reaction towards oTarget, depending
on the reputation, PVP setting and (if both oSource and oTarget are PCs),
oSource's Like/Dislike setting for oTarget.
Note: If you just want to know how two objects feel about each other in terms
of faction and personal reputation, use GetIsFriend() instead.
Returns TRUE if oSource has a friendly reaction towards oTarget

functions.name: GetIsReactionTypeNeutral
functions.signature: int GetIsReactionTypeNeutral(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determine whether oSource has a neutral reaction towards oTarget, depending
on the reputation, PVP setting and (if both oSource and oTarget are PCs),
oSource's Like/Dislike setting for oTarget.
Note: If you just want to know how two objects feel about each other in terms
of faction and personal reputation, use GetIsNeutral() instead.
Returns TRUE if oSource has a neutral reaction towards oTarget

functions.name: GetIsReactionTypeHostile
functions.signature: int GetIsReactionTypeHostile(object oTarget, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Determine whether oSource has a Hostile reaction towards oTarget, depending
on the reputation, PVP setting and (if both oSource and oTarget are PCs),
oSource's Like/Dislike setting for oTarget.
Note: If you just want to know how two objects feel about each other in terms
of faction and personal reputation, use GetIsEnemy() instead.
Returns TRUE if oSource has a hostile reaction towards oTarget

functions.name: EffectSpellLevelAbsorption
functions.params: int nMaxSpellLevelAbsorbed, int nTotalSpellLevelsAbsorbed = 0, int nSpellSchool = SPELL_SCHOOL_GENERAL
functions.signature: effect EffectSpellLevelAbsorption(int nMaxSpellLevelAbsorbed, int nTotalSpellLevelsAbsorbed = 0, int nSpellSchool = SPELL_SCHOOL_GENERAL)
doc: Create a Spell Level Absorption effect.
- nMaxSpellLevelAbsorbed: maximum spell level that will be absorbed by the
effect
- nTotalSpellLevelsAbsorbed: maximum number of spell levels that will be
absorbed by the effect
- nSpellSchool: SPELL_SCHOOL_*
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if:
nMaxSpellLevelAbsorbed is not between -1 and 9 inclusive, or nSpellSchool
is invalid.

functions.name: EffectDispelMagicBest
doc: Create a Dispel Magic Best effect.
If no parameter is specified, USE_CREATURE_LEVEL will be used. This will
cause the dispel effect to use the level of the creature that created the
effect.

functions.name: ActivatePortal
functions.params: object oTarget, string sIPaddress = "", string sPassword = "", string sWaypointTag = "", int bSeemless = FALSE
functions.signature: void ActivatePortal(object oTarget, string sIPaddress = "", string sPassword = "", string sWaypointTag = "", int bSeemless = FALSE)
doc: Try to send oTarget to a new server defined by sIPaddress.
- oTarget
- sIPaddress: this can be numerical "192.168.0.84" or alphanumeric
"www.bioware.com". It can also contain a port "192.168.0.84:5121" or
"www.bioware.com:5121"; if the port is not specified, it will default to
- sPassword: login password for the destination server
- sWaypointTag: if this is set, after portalling the character will be moved
to this waypoint if it exists
- bSeamless: if this is set, the client wil not be prompted with the
information window telling them about the server, and they will not be
allowed to save a copy of their character if they are using a local vault
character.

functions.name: GetNumStackedItems
doc: Get the number of stacked items that oItem comprises.

functions.name: SurrenderToEnemies
doc: Use this on an NPC to cause all creatures within a 10-metre radius to stop
what they are doing and sets the NPC's enemies within this range to be
neutral towards the NPC for roughly 3 minutes. If this command is run on a PC
or an object that is not a creature, nothing will happen.

functions.name: EffectMissChance
functions.signature: effect EffectMissChance(int nPercentage, int nMissChanceType = MISS_CHANCE_TYPE_NORMAL)
doc: Create a Miss Chance effect.
- nPercentage: 1-100 inclusive
- nMissChanceType: MISS_CHANCE_TYPE_*
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nPercentage < 1 or
nPercentage > 100.

functions.name: GetTurnResistanceHD
doc: Get the number of Hitdice worth of Turn Resistance that oUndead may have.
This will only work on undead creatures.

functions.name: GetCreatureSize
doc: Get the size (CREATURE_SIZE_*) of oCreature.

functions.name: EffectDisappearAppear
doc: Create a Disappear/Appear effect.
The object will "fly away" for the duration of the effect and will reappear
at lLocation.
- nAnimation determines which appear and disappear animations to use. Most creatures
only have animation 1, although a few have 2 (like beholders)

functions.name: EffectDisappear
doc: Create a Disappear effect to make the object "fly away" and then destroy
itself.
- nAnimation determines which appear and disappear animations to use. Most creatures
only have animation 1, although a few have 2 (like beholders)

functions.name: EffectAppear
doc: Create an Appear effect to make the object "fly in".
- nAnimation determines which appear and disappear animations to use. Most creatures
only have animation 1, although a few have 2 (like beholders)

functions.name: ActionUnlockObject
doc: The action subject will unlock oTarget, which can be a door or a placeable
object.

functions.name: ActionLockObject
doc: The action subject will lock oTarget, which can be a door or a placeable
object.

functions.name: EffectModifyAttacks
doc: Create a Modify Attacks effect to add attacks.
- nAttacks: maximum is 5, even with the effect stacked
Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_INVALIDEFFECT if nAttacks > 5.

functions.name: GetLastTrapDetected
doc: Get the last trap detected by oTarget.
Return value on error: OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: EffectDamageShield
functions.params: int nDamageAmount, int nRandomAmount, int nDamageType
functions.signature: effect EffectDamageShield(int nDamageAmount, int nRandomAmount, int nDamageType)
doc: Create a Damage Shield effect which does (nDamageAmount + nRandomAmount)
damage to any melee attacker on a successful attack of damage type nDamageType.
- nDamageAmount: an integer value
- nRandomAmount: DAMAGE_BONUS_*
- nDamageType: DAMAGE_TYPE_*
NOTE! You *must* use the DAMAGE_BONUS_* constants! Using other values may
result in odd behaviour.

functions.name: GetNearestTrapToObject
functions.params: object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nTrapDetected = TRUE
functions.signature: object GetNearestTrapToObject(object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int nTrapDetected = TRUE)
doc: Get the trap nearest to oTarget.
Note : "trap objects" are actually any trigger, placeable or door that is
trapped in oTarget's area.
- oTarget
- nTrapDetected: if this is TRUE, the trap returned has to have been detected
by oTarget.

functions.name: GetDeity
doc: Get the name of oCreature's deity.
Returns "" if oCreature is invalid (or if the deity name is blank for
oCreature).

functions.name: GetSubRace
doc: Get the name of oCreature's sub race.
Returns "" if oCreature is invalid (or if sub race is blank for oCreature).

functions.name: GetFortitudeSavingThrow
doc: Get oTarget's base fortitude saving throw value (this will only work for
creatures, doors, and placeables).
Returns 0 if oTarget is invalid.

functions.name: GetWillSavingThrow
doc: Get oTarget's base will saving throw value (this will only work for creatures,
doors, and placeables).
Returns 0 if oTarget is invalid.

functions.name: GetReflexSavingThrow
doc: Get oTarget's base reflex saving throw value (this will only work for
creatures, doors, and placeables).
Returns 0 if oTarget is invalid.

functions.name: GetChallengeRating
doc: Get oCreature's challenge rating.
Returns 0.0 if oCreature is invalid.

functions.name: GetAge
doc: Get oCreature's age.
Returns 0 if oCreature is invalid.

functions.name: GetMovementRate
doc: Get oCreature's movement rate.
Returns 0 if oCreature is invalid.

functions.name: GetFamiliarCreatureType
doc: Get oCreature's familiar creature type (FAMILIAR_CREATURE_TYPE_*).
Returns FAMILIAR_CREATURE_TYPE_NONE if oCreature is invalid or does not
currently have a familiar.

functions.name: GetAnimalCompanionCreatureType
doc: Get oCreature's animal companion creature type
(ANIMAL_COMPANION_CREATURE_TYPE_*).
Returns ANIMAL_COMPANION_CREATURE_TYPE_NONE if oCreature is invalid or does
not currently have an animal companion.

functions.name: GetFamiliarName
doc: Get oCreature's familiar's name.
Returns "" if oCreature is invalid, does not currently
have a familiar or if the familiar's name is blank.

functions.name: GetAnimalCompanionName
doc: Get oCreature's animal companion's name.
Returns "" if oCreature is invalid, does not currently
have an animal companion or if the animal companion's name is blank.

functions.name: ActionCastFakeSpellAtObject
functions.params: int nSpell, object oTarget, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT
functions.signature: void ActionCastFakeSpellAtObject(int nSpell, object oTarget, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT)
doc: The action subject will fake casting a spell at oTarget; the conjure and cast
animations and visuals will occur, nothing else.
- nSpell
- oTarget
- nProjectilePathType: PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_*

functions.name: ActionCastFakeSpellAtLocation
functions.params: int nSpell, location lTarget, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT
functions.signature: void ActionCastFakeSpellAtLocation(int nSpell, location lTarget, int nProjectilePathType = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT)
doc: The action subject will fake casting a spell at lLocation; the conjure and
cast animations and visuals will occur, nothing else.
- nSpell
- lTarget
- nProjectilePathType: PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_*

functions.name: RemoveSummonedAssociate
functions.signature: void RemoveSummonedAssociate(object oMaster, object oAssociate = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Removes oAssociate from the service of oMaster, returning them to their
original faction.

functions.name: SetCameraMode
doc: Set the camera mode for oPlayer.
- oPlayer
- nCameraMode: CAMERA_MODE_*
If oPlayer is not player-controlled or nCameraMode is invalid, nothing
happens.

functions.name: GetIsResting
doc: Returns TRUE if oCreature is resting.

functions.name: GetLastPCRested
doc: Get the last PC that has rested in the module.

functions.name: SetWeather
doc: Set the weather for oTarget.
- oTarget: if this is GetModule(), all outdoor areas will be modified by the
weather constant. If it is an area, oTarget will play the weather only if
it is an outdoor area.
- nWeather: WEATHER_*
-> WEATHER_USER_AREA_SETTINGS will set the area back to random weather.
-> WEATHER_CLEAR, WEATHER_RAIN, WEATHER_SNOW will make the weather go to
the appropriate precipitation *without stopping*.

functions.name: GetLastRestEventType
doc: Determine the type (REST_EVENTTYPE_REST_*) of the last rest event (as
returned from the OnPCRested module event).

functions.name: StartNewModule
doc: Shut down the currently loaded module and start a new one (moving all
currently-connected players to the starting point.

functions.name: EffectSwarm
functions.params: int nLooping, string sCreatureTemplate1, string sCreatureTemplate2 = "", string sCreatureTemplate3 = "", string sCreatureTemplate4 = ""
functions.signature: effect EffectSwarm(int nLooping, string sCreatureTemplate1, string sCreatureTemplate2 = "", string sCreatureTemplate3 = "", string sCreatureTemplate4 = "")
doc: Create a Swarm effect.
- nLooping: If this is TRUE, for the duration of the effect when one creature
created by this effect dies, the next one in the list will be created.  If
the last creature in the list dies, we loop back to the beginning and
sCreatureTemplate1 will be created, and so on...
- sCreatureTemplate1
- sCreatureTemplate2
- sCreatureTemplate3
- sCreatureTemplate4

functions.name: GetWeaponRanged
doc: Returns TRUE if oItem is a ranged weapon.

functions.name: DoSinglePlayerAutoSave
doc: Only if we are in a single player game, AutoSave the game.

functions.name: GetGameDifficulty
doc: Get the game difficulty (GAME_DIFFICULTY_*).

functions.name: SetTileMainLightColor
functions.params: location lTileLocation, int nMainLight1Color, int nMainLight2Color
functions.signature: void SetTileMainLightColor(location lTileLocation, int nMainLight1Color, int nMainLight2Color)
doc: Set the main light color on the tile at lTileLocation.
- lTileLocation: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of
the tile.
- nMainLight1Color: TILE_MAIN_LIGHT_COLOR_*
- nMainLight2Color: TILE_MAIN_LIGHT_COLOR_*

functions.name: SetTileSourceLightColor
functions.params: location lTileLocation, int nSourceLight1Color, int nSourceLight2Color
functions.signature: void SetTileSourceLightColor(location lTileLocation, int nSourceLight1Color, int nSourceLight2Color)
doc: Set the source light color on the tile at lTileLocation.
- lTileLocation: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of
the tile.
- nSourceLight1Color: TILE_SOURCE_LIGHT_COLOR_*
- nSourceLight2Color: TILE_SOURCE_LIGHT_COLOR_*

functions.name: RecomputeStaticLighting
doc: All clients in oArea will recompute the static lighting.
This can be used to update the lighting after changing any tile lights or if
placeables with lights have been added/deleted.

functions.name: GetTileMainLight1Color
doc: Get the color (TILE_MAIN_LIGHT_COLOR_*) for the main light 1 of the tile at
lTile.
- lTile: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of the tile.

functions.name: GetTileMainLight2Color
doc: Get the color (TILE_MAIN_LIGHT_COLOR_*) for the main light 2 of the tile at
lTile.
- lTile: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of the
tile.

functions.name: GetTileSourceLight1Color
doc: Get the color (TILE_SOURCE_LIGHT_COLOR_*) for the source light 1 of the tile
at lTile.
- lTile: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of the
tile.

functions.name: GetTileSourceLight2Color
doc: Get the color (TILE_SOURCE_LIGHT_COLOR_*) for the source light 2 of the tile
at lTile.
- lTile: the vector part of this is the tile grid (x,y) coordinate of the
tile.

functions.name: SetPanelButtonFlash
functions.params: object oPlayer, int nButton, int nEnableFlash
functions.signature: void SetPanelButtonFlash(object oPlayer, int nButton, int nEnableFlash)
doc: Make the corresponding panel button on the player's client start or stop
flashing.
- oPlayer
- nButton: PANEL_BUTTON_*
- nEnableFlash: if this is TRUE nButton will start flashing.  It if is FALSE,
nButton will stop flashing.

functions.name: GetCurrentAction
doc: Get the current action (ACTION_*) that oObject is executing.

functions.name: SetStandardFactionReputation
functions.params: int nStandardFaction, int nNewReputation, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF
functions.signature: void SetStandardFactionReputation(int nStandardFaction, int nNewReputation, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Set how nStandardFaction feels about oCreature.
- nStandardFaction: STANDARD_FACTION_*
- nNewReputation: 0-100 (inclusive)
- oCreature

functions.name: GetStandardFactionReputation
functions.signature: int GetStandardFactionReputation(int nStandardFaction, object oCreature = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Find out how nStandardFaction feels about oCreature.
- nStandardFaction: STANDARD_FACTION_*
- oCreature
Returns -1 on an error.
Returns 0-100 based on the standing of oCreature within the faction nStandardFaction.
0-10   :  Hostile.
11-89  :  Neutral.
90-100 :  Friendly.

functions.name: FloatingTextStrRefOnCreature
functions.params: int nStrRefToDisplay, object oCreatureToFloatAbove, int bBroadcastToFaction = TRUE, int bChatWindow = TRUE
functions.signature: void FloatingTextStrRefOnCreature(int nStrRefToDisplay, object oCreatureToFloatAbove, int bBroadcastToFaction = TRUE, int bChatWindow = TRUE)
doc: Display floaty text above the specified creature.
The text will also appear in the chat buffer of each player that receives the
floaty text.
- nStrRefToDisplay: String ref (therefore text is translated)
- oCreatureToFloatAbove
- bBroadcastToFaction: If this is TRUE then only creatures in the same faction
as oCreatureToFloatAbove
will see the floaty text, and only if they are within range (30 metres).
- bChatWindow:  If TRUE, the string reference will be displayed in oCreatureToFloatAbove's chat window

functions.name: FloatingTextStringOnCreature
functions.params: string sStringToDisplay, object oCreatureToFloatAbove, int bBroadcastToFaction = TRUE, int bChatWindow = TRUE
functions.signature: void FloatingTextStringOnCreature(string sStringToDisplay, object oCreatureToFloatAbove, int bBroadcastToFaction = TRUE, int bChatWindow = TRUE)
doc: Display floaty text above the specified creature.
The text will also appear in the chat buffer of each player that receives the
floaty text.
- sStringToDisplay: String
- oCreatureToFloatAbove
- bBroadcastToFaction: If this is TRUE then only creatures in the same faction
as oCreatureToFloatAbove
will see the floaty text, and only if they are within range (30 metres).
- bChatWindow:  If TRUE, sStringToDisplay will be displayed in oCreatureToFloatAbove's chat window.

functions.name: GetTrapDisarmable
doc: - oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
Returns TRUE if oTrapObject is disarmable.

functions.name: GetTrapDetectable
doc: - oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
Returns TRUE if oTrapObject is detectable.

functions.name: GetTrapDetectedBy
doc: - oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
- oCreature
Returns TRUE if oCreature has detected oTrapObject

functions.name: GetTrapFlagged
doc: - oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
Returns TRUE if oTrapObject has been flagged as visible to all creatures.

functions.name: GetTrapBaseType
doc: Get the trap base type (TRAP_BASE_TYPE_*) of oTrapObject.
- oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger

functions.name: GetTrapOneShot
doc: - oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
Returns TRUE if oTrapObject is one-shot (i.e. it does not reset itself
after firing.

functions.name: GetTrapCreator
doc: Get the creator of oTrapObject, the creature that set the trap.
- oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oTrapObject was created in the toolset.

functions.name: GetTrapKeyTag
doc: Get the tag of the key that will disarm oTrapObject.
- oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger

functions.name: GetTrapDisarmDC
doc: Get the DC for disarming oTrapObject.
- oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger

functions.name: GetTrapDetectDC
doc: Get the DC for detecting oTrapObject.
- oTrapObject: a placeable, door or trigger

functions.name: GetLockKeyRequired
doc: Returns TRUE if a specific key is required to open the lock on oObject.

functions.name: GetLockKeyTag
doc: Get the tag of the key that will open the lock on oObject.

functions.name: GetLockLockable
doc: Returns TRUE if the lock on oObject is lockable.

functions.name: GetLockUnlockDC
doc: Get the DC for unlocking oObject.

functions.name: GetLockLockDC
doc: Get the DC for locking oObject.

functions.name: GetPCLevellingUp
doc: Get the last PC that levelled up.

functions.name: GetHasFeatEffect
functions.signature: int GetHasFeatEffect(int nFeat, object oObject = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: - nFeat: FEAT_*
- oObject
Returns TRUE if oObject has effects on it originating from nFeat.

functions.name: SetPlaceableIllumination
functions.params: object oPlaceable = OBJECT_SELF, int bIlluminate = TRUE
functions.signature: void SetPlaceableIllumination(object oPlaceable = OBJECT_SELF, int bIlluminate = TRUE)
doc: Set the status of the illumination for oPlaceable.
- oPlaceable
- bIlluminate: if this is TRUE, oPlaceable's illumination will be turned on.
If this is FALSE, oPlaceable's illumination will be turned off.
Note: You must call RecomputeStaticLighting() after calling this function in
order for the changes to occur visually for the players.
SetPlaceableIllumination() buffers the illumination changes, which are then
sent out to the players once RecomputeStaticLighting() is called.  As such,
it is best to call SetPlaceableIllumination() for all the placeables you wish
to set the illumination on, and then call RecomputeStaticLighting() once after
all the placeable illumination has been set.
If oPlaceable is not a placeable object, or oPlaceable is a placeable that
doesn't have a light, nothing will happen.

functions.name: GetPlaceableIllumination
doc: Returns TRUE if the illumination for oPlaceable is on

functions.name: GetIsPlaceableObjectActionPossible
doc: - oPlaceable
- nPlaceableAction: PLACEABLE_ACTION_*
Returns TRUE if nPlacebleAction is valid for oPlaceable.

functions.name: DoPlaceableObjectAction
doc: The caller performs nPlaceableAction on oPlaceable.
- oPlaceable
- nPlaceableAction: PLACEABLE_ACTION_*

functions.name: GetFirstPC
doc: Get the first PC in the player list.
This resets the position in the player list for GetNextPC().

functions.name: GetNextPC
doc: Get the next PC in the player list.
This picks up where the last GetFirstPC() or GetNextPC() left off.

functions.name: SetTrapDetectedBy
functions.params: object oTrap, object oDetector, int bDetected = TRUE
functions.signature: int SetTrapDetectedBy(object oTrap, object oDetector, int bDetected = TRUE)
doc: Set whether or not the creature oDetector has detected the trapped object oTrap.
- oTrap: A trapped trigger, placeable or door object.
- oDetector: This is the creature that the detected status of the trap is being adjusted for.
- bDetected: A Boolean that sets whether the trapped object has been detected or not.

functions.name: GetIsTrapped
doc: Note: Only placeables, doors and triggers can be trapped.
Returns TRUE if oObject is trapped.

functions.name: EffectTurnResistanceDecrease
doc: Create a Turn Resistance Decrease effect.
- nHitDice: a positive number representing the number of hit dice for the
decrease

functions.name: EffectTurnResistanceIncrease
doc: Create a Turn Resistance Increase effect.
- nHitDice: a positive number representing the number of hit dice for the
increase

functions.name: PopUpDeathGUIPanel
functions.params: object oPC, int bRespawnButtonEnabled = TRUE, int bWaitForHelpButtonEnabled = TRUE, int nHelpStringReference = 0, string sHelpString = ""
functions.signature: void PopUpDeathGUIPanel(object oPC, int bRespawnButtonEnabled = TRUE, int bWaitForHelpButtonEnabled = TRUE, int nHelpStringReference = 0, string sHelpString = "")
doc: Spawn in the Death GUI.
The default (as defined by BioWare) can be spawned in by PopUpGUIPanel, but
if you want to turn off the "Respawn" or "Wait for Help" buttons, this is the
function to use.
- oPC
- bRespawnButtonEnabled: if this is TRUE, the "Respawn" button will be enabled
on the Death GUI.
- bWaitForHelpButtonEnabled: if this is TRUE, the "Wait For Help" button will
be enabled on the Death GUI (Note: This button will not appear in single player games).
- nHelpStringReference
- sHelpString

functions.name: SetTrapDisabled
doc: Disable oTrap.
- oTrap: a placeable, door or trigger.

functions.name: GetLastHostileActor
doc: Get the last object that was sent as a GetLastAttacker(), GetLastDamager(),
GetLastSpellCaster() (for a hostile spell), or GetLastDisturbed() (when a
creature is pickpocketed).
Note: Return values may only ever be:
1) A Creature
2) Plot Characters will never have this value set
3) Area of Effect Objects will return the AOE creator if they are registered
as this value, otherwise they will return INVALID_OBJECT_ID
4) Traps will not return the creature that set the trap.
5) This value will never be overwritten by another non-creature object.
6) This value will never be a dead/destroyed creature

functions.name: ExportAllCharacters
doc: Force all the characters of the players who are currently in the game to
be exported to their respective directories i.e. LocalVault/ServerVault/ etc.

functions.name: MusicBackgroundGetDayTrack
doc: Get the Day Track for oArea.

functions.name: MusicBackgroundGetNightTrack
doc: Get the Night Track for oArea.

functions.name: WriteTimestampedLogEntry
doc: Write sLogEntry as a timestamped entry into the log file

functions.name: GetModuleName
doc: Get the module's name in the language of the server that's running it.
If there is no entry for the language of the server, it will return an
empty string

functions.name: GetFactionLeader
doc: Get the player leader of the faction of which oMemberOfFaction is a member.
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if oMemberOfFaction is not a valid creature,
or oMemberOfFaction is a member of a NPC faction.

functions.name: SendMessageToAllDMs
doc: Sends szMessage to all the Dungeon Masters currently on the server.

functions.name: EndGame
doc: End the currently running game, play sEndMovie then return all players to the
game's main menu.

functions.name: BootPC
doc: Remove oPlayer from the server.
You can optionally specify a reason to override the text shown to the player.

functions.name: ActionCounterSpell
doc: Counterspell oCounterSpellTarget.

functions.name: AmbientSoundSetDayVolume
doc: Set the ambient day volume for oArea to nVolume.
- oArea
- nVolume: 0 - 100

functions.name: AmbientSoundSetNightVolume
doc: Set the ambient night volume for oArea to nVolume.
- oArea
- nVolume: 0 - 100

functions.name: MusicBackgroundGetBattleTrack
doc: Get the Battle Track for oArea.

functions.name: GetHasInventory
doc: Determine whether oObject has an inventory.
Returns TRUE for creatures and stores, and checks to see if an item or placeable object is a container.
Returns FALSE for all other object types.

functions.name: GetStrRefSoundDuration
doc: Get the duration (in seconds) of the sound attached to nStrRef
Returns 0.0f if no duration is stored or if no sound is attached

functions.name: AddToParty
doc: Add oPC to oPartyLeader's party.  This will only work on two PCs.
- oPC: player to add to a party
- oPartyLeader: player already in the party

functions.name: RemoveFromParty
doc: Remove oPC from their current party. This will only work on a PC.
- oPC: removes this player from whatever party they're currently in.

functions.name: GetStealthMode
doc: Returns the stealth mode of the specified creature.
- oCreature
Returns a constant STEALTH_MODE_*

functions.name: GetDetectMode
doc: Returns the detection mode of the specified creature.
- oCreature
Returns a constant DETECT_MODE_*

functions.name: GetDefensiveCastingMode
doc: Returns the defensive casting mode of the specified creature.
- oCreature
Returns a constant DEFENSIVE_CASTING_MODE_*

functions.name: GetAppearanceType
doc: returns the appearance type of the specified creature.
returns a constant APPEARANCE_TYPE_* for valid creatures
returns APPEARANCE_TYPE_INVALID for non creatures/invalid creatures

functions.name: SpawnScriptDebugger
doc: SpawnScriptDebugger() will attempt to communicate with the a running script debugger
instance. You need to run it yourself, and enable it in Options/Config beforehand.
A sample debug server is included with the game installation in utils/.
Will only work in singleplayer, NOT on dedicated servers.
In order to compile the script for debugging go to Tools->Options->Script Editor
and check the box labeled "Generate Debug Information When Compiling Scripts"
After you have checked the above box, recompile the script that you want to debug.
If the script file isn't compiled for debugging, this command will do nothing.
Remove any SpawnScriptDebugger() calls once you have finished
debugging the script.

functions.name: GetModuleItemAcquiredStackSize
doc: in an onItemAcquired script, returns the size of the stack of the item
that was just acquired.
returns the stack size of the item acquired

functions.name: DecrementRemainingFeatUses
doc: Decrement the remaining uses per day for this creature by one.
- oCreature: creature to modify
- nFeat: constant FEAT_*

functions.name: DecrementRemainingSpellUses
doc: Decrement the remaining uses per day for this creature by one.
- oCreature: creature to modify
- nSpell: constant SPELL_*

functions.name: GetResRef
doc: returns the template used to create this object (if appropriate)
returns an empty string when no template found

functions.name: EffectPetrify
doc: returns an effect that will petrify the target
currently applies EffectParalyze and the stoneskin visual effect.

functions.name: CopyItem
functions.params: object oItem, object oTargetInventory = OBJECT_INVALID, int bCopyVars = FALSE
functions.signature: object CopyItem(object oItem, object oTargetInventory = OBJECT_INVALID, int bCopyVars = FALSE)
doc: duplicates the item and returns a new object
oItem - item to copy
oTargetInventory - create item in this object's inventory. If this parameter
is not valid, the item will be created in oItem's location
bCopyVars - copy the local variables from the old item to the new one
returns the new item
returns OBJECT_INVALID for non-items.
can only copy empty item containers. will return OBJECT_INVALID if oItem contains
other items.
if it is possible to merge this item with any others in the target location,
then it will do so and return the merged object.

functions.name: EffectCutsceneParalyze
doc: returns an effect that is guaranteed to paralyze a creature.
this effect is identical to EffectParalyze except that it cannot be resisted.

functions.name: GetDroppableFlag
doc: returns TRUE if the item CAN be dropped
Droppable items will appear on a creature's remains when the creature is killed.

functions.name: GetUseableFlag
doc: returns TRUE if the object is usable

functions.name: GetStolenFlag
doc: returns TRUE if the item is stolen

functions.name: SetCampaignFloat
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, float flFloat, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID
functions.signature: void SetCampaignFloat(string sCampaignName, string sVarName, float flFloat, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID)
doc: This stores a float out to the specified campaign database
The database name:
- is case insensitive and it must be the same for both set and get functions.
- can only contain alphanumeric characters, no spaces.
The var name must be unique across the entire database, regardless of the variable type.
If you want a variable to pertain to a specific player in the game, provide a player object.

functions.name: SetCampaignInt
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, int nInt, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID
functions.signature: void SetCampaignInt(string sCampaignName, string sVarName, int nInt, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID)

functions.name: DestroyCampaignDatabase
doc: This will delete the entire campaign database if it exists.

functions.name: GetCampaignString
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID

functions.name: CopyObject
functions.params: object oSource, location locLocation, object oOwner = OBJECT_INVALID, string sNewTag = "", int bCopyLocalState = FALSE
functions.signature: object CopyObject(object oSource, location locLocation, object oOwner = OBJECT_INVALID, string sNewTag = "", int bCopyLocalState = FALSE)
doc: Duplicates the object specified by oSource.
NOTE: this command can be used for copying Creatures, Items, Placeables, Waypoints, Stores, Doors, Triggers, Encounters.
If an owner is specified and the object is an item, it will be put into their inventory
Otherwise, it will be created at the location.
If a new tag is specified, it will be assigned to the new object.
If bCopyLocalState is TRUE, local vars, effects, action queue, and transition info (triggers, doors) are copied over.

functions.name: DeleteCampaignVariable
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID
functions.signature: void DeleteCampaignVariable(string sCampaignName, string sVarName, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID)
doc: This will remove ANY campaign variable. Regardless of type.

functions.name: StoreCampaignObject
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, object oObject, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID, int bSaveObjectState = FALSE
functions.signature: int StoreCampaignObject(string sCampaignName, string sVarName, object oObject, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID, int bSaveObjectState = FALSE)
doc: Stores an object with the given id.
NOTE: this command can be used for storing Creatures, Items, Placeables, Waypoints, Stores, Doors, Triggers, Encounters.
Returns 0 if it failled, 1 if it worked.
If bSaveObjectState is TRUE, local vars, effects, action queue, and transition info (triggers, doors) are saved out
(except for Combined Area Format, which always has object state saved out).

functions.name: RetrieveCampaignObject
functions.params: string sCampaignName, string sVarName, location locLocation, object oOwner = OBJECT_INVALID, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID, int bLoadObjectState = FALSE
functions.signature: object RetrieveCampaignObject(string sCampaignName, string sVarName, location locLocation, object oOwner = OBJECT_INVALID, object oPlayer = OBJECT_INVALID, int bLoadObjectState = FALSE)
doc: Use RetrieveCampaign with the given id to restore it.
If you specify an owner, the object will try to be created in their repository
If the owner can't handle the item (or if it's a non-item) it will be created at the given location.
If bLoadObjectState is TRUE, local vars, effects, action queue, and transition info (triggers, doors) are read in.

functions.name: EffectCutsceneDominated
doc: Returns an effect that is guaranteed to dominate a creature
Like EffectDominated but cannot be resisted

functions.name: GetItemStackSize
doc: Returns stack size of an item
- oItem: item to query

functions.name: SetItemStackSize
doc: Sets stack size of an item.
- oItem: item to change
- nSize: new size of stack.  Will be restricted to be between 1 and the
maximum stack size for the item type.  If a value less than 1 is passed it
will set the stack to 1.  If a value greater than the max is passed
then it will set the stack to the maximum size

functions.name: GetItemCharges
doc: Returns charges left on an item
- oItem: item to query

functions.name: SetItemCharges
doc: Sets charges left on an item.
- oItem: item to change
- nCharges: number of charges.  If value below 0 is passed, # charges will
be set to 0.  If value greater than maximum is passed, # charges will
be set to maximum.  If the # charges drops to 0 the item
will be destroyed.

functions.name: AddItemProperty
functions.params: int nDurationType, itemproperty ipProperty, object oItem, float fDuration = 0.0f
functions.signature: void AddItemProperty(int nDurationType, itemproperty ipProperty, object oItem, float fDuration = 0.0f)
doc: adds an item property to the specified item
Only temporary and permanent duration types are allowed.

functions.name: RemoveItemProperty
doc: removes an item property from the specified item

functions.name: GetIsItemPropertyValid
doc: if the item property is valid this will return true

functions.name: GetFirstItemProperty
doc: Gets the first item property on an item

functions.name: GetNextItemProperty
doc: Will keep retrieving the next and the next item property on an Item,
will return an invalid item property when the list is empty.

functions.name: GetItemPropertyType
doc: will return the item property type (ie. holy avenger)

functions.name: GetItemPropertyDurationType
doc: will return the duration type of the item property

functions.name: ItemPropertyAbilityBonus
doc: Returns Item property ability bonus.  You need to specify an
ability constant(IP_CONST_ABILITY_*) and the bonus.  The bonus should
be a positive integer between 1 and 12.

functions.name: ItemPropertyACBonus
doc: Returns Item property AC bonus.  You need to specify the bonus.
The bonus should be a positive integer between 1 and 20. The modifier
type depends on the item it is being applied to.

functions.name: ItemPropertyACBonusVsAlign
doc: Returns Item property AC bonus vs. alignment group.  An example of
an alignment group is Chaotic, or Good.  You need to specify the
alignment group constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*) and the AC bonus.
The AC bonus should be an integer between 1 and 20.  The modifier
type depends on the item it is being applied to.

functions.name: ItemPropertyACBonusVsDmgType
doc: Returns Item property AC bonus vs. Damage type (ie. piercing).  You
need to specify the damage type constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the
AC bonus.  The AC bonus should be an integer between 1 and 20.  The
modifier type depends on the item it is being applied to.
NOTE: Only the first 3 damage types may be used here, the 3 basic
physical types.

functions.name: ItemPropertyACBonusVsRace
doc: Returns Item property AC bonus vs. Racial group.  You need to specify
the racial group constant(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*) and the AC bonus.  The AC
bonus should be an integer between 1 and 20.  The modifier type depends
on the item it is being applied to.

functions.name: ItemPropertyACBonusVsSAlign
doc: Returns Item property AC bonus vs. Specific alignment.  You need to
specify the specific alignment constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*) and the AC
bonus.  The AC bonus should be an integer between 1 and 20.  The
modifier type depends on the item it is being applied to.

functions.name: ItemPropertyEnhancementBonus
doc: Returns Item property Enhancement bonus.  You need to specify the
enhancement bonus.  The Enhancement bonus should be an integer between
1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyEnhancementBonusVsAlign
doc: Returns Item property Enhancement bonus vs. an Alignment group.  You
need to specify the alignment group constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*)
and the enhancement bonus.  The Enhancement bonus should be an integer
between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyEnhancementBonusVsRace
doc: Returns Item property Enhancement bonus vs. Racial group.  You need
to specify the racial group constant(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*) and the
enhancement bonus.  The enhancement bonus should be an integer between
1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyEnhancementBonusVsSAlign
doc: Returns Item property Enhancement bonus vs. a specific alignment.  You
need to specify the alignment constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*) and the
enhancement bonus.  The enhancement bonus should be an integer between
1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyEnhancementPenalty
doc: Returns Item property Enhancment penalty.  You need to specify the
enhancement penalty.  The enhancement penalty should be a POSITIVE
integer between 1 and 5 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyWeightReduction
doc: Returns Item property weight reduction.  You need to specify the weight
reduction constant(IP_CONST_REDUCEDWEIGHT_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyBonusFeat
doc: Returns Item property Bonus Feat.  You need to specify the the feat
constant(IP_CONST_FEAT_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyBonusLevelSpell
doc: Returns Item property Bonus level spell (Bonus spell of level).  You must
specify the class constant(IP_CONST_CLASS_*) of the bonus spell(MUST BE a
spell casting class) and the level of the bonus spell.  The level of the
bonus spell should be an integer between 0 and 9.

functions.name: ItemPropertyCastSpell
doc: Returns Item property Cast spell.  You must specify the spell constant
(IP_CONST_CASTSPELL_*) and the number of uses constant(IP_CONST_CASTSPELL_NUMUSES_*).
NOTE: The number after the name of the spell in the constant is the level
at which the spell will be cast.  Sometimes there are multiple copies
of the same spell but they each are cast at a different level.  The higher
the level, the more cost will be added to the item.
NOTE: The list of spells that can be applied to an item will depend on the
item type.  For instance there are spells that can be applied to a wand
that cannot be applied to a potion.  Below is a list of the types and the
spells that are allowed to be placed on them.  If you try to put a cast
spell effect on an item that is not allowed to have that effect it will
not work.
NOTE: Even if spells have multiple versions of different levels they are only
listed below once.

GENERAL USE (ie. everything else):
Just about every spell is useable by all the general use items so instead we
will only list the ones that are not allowed:
Special_Alcohol_Beer
Special_Alcohol_Spirits
Special_Alcohol_Wine

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageBonus
doc: Returns Item property damage bonus.  You must specify the damage type constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the amount of damage constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEBONUS_*).
NOTE: not all the damage types will work, use only the following: Acid, Bludgeoning,
Cold, Electrical, Fire, Piercing, Slashing, Sonic.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsAlign
functions.params: int nAlignGroup, int nDamageType, int nDamage
functions.signature: itemproperty ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsAlign(int nAlignGroup, int nDamageType, int nDamage)
doc: Returns Item property damage bonus vs. Alignment groups.  You must specify the
alignment group constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*) and the damage type constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the amount of damage constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEBONUS_*).
NOTE: not all the damage types will work, use only the following: Acid, Bludgeoning,
Cold, Electrical, Fire, Piercing, Slashing, Sonic.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsRace
functions.params: int nRace, int nDamageType, int nDamage
functions.signature: itemproperty ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsRace(int nRace, int nDamageType, int nDamage)
doc: Returns Item property damage bonus vs. specific race.  You must specify the
racial group constant(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*) and the damage type constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the amount of damage constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEBONUS_*).
NOTE: not all the damage types will work, use only the following: Acid, Bludgeoning,
Cold, Electrical, Fire, Piercing, Slashing, Sonic.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsSAlign
functions.params: int nAlign, int nDamageType, int nDamage
functions.signature: itemproperty ItemPropertyDamageBonusVsSAlign(int nAlign, int nDamageType, int nDamage)
doc: Returns Item property damage bonus vs. specific alignment.  You must specify the
specific alignment constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*) and the damage type constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the amount of damage constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEBONUS_*).
NOTE: not all the damage types will work, use only the following: Acid, Bludgeoning,
Cold, Electrical, Fire, Piercing, Slashing, Sonic.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageImmunity
doc: Returns Item property damage immunity.  You must specify the damage type constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) that you want to be immune to and the immune bonus percentage
constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEIMMUNITY_*).
NOTE: not all the damage types will work, use only the following: Acid, Bludgeoning,
Cold, Electrical, Fire, Piercing, Slashing, Sonic.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamagePenalty
doc: Returns Item property damage penalty.  You must specify the damage penalty.
The damage penalty should be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 5 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageReduction
doc: Returns Item property damage reduction.  You must specify the enhancment level
(IP_CONST_DAMAGEREDUCTION_*) that is required to get past the damage reduction
and the amount of HP of damage constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGESOAK_*) will be soaked
up if your weapon is not of high enough enhancement.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageResistance
doc: Returns Item property damage resistance.  You must specify the damage type
constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) and the amount of HP of damage constant
(IP_CONST_DAMAGERESIST_*) that will be resisted against each round.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDamageVulnerability
doc: Returns Item property damage vulnerability.  You must specify the damage type
constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*) that you want the user to be extra vulnerable to
and the percentage vulnerability constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEVULNERABILITY_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyDarkvision
doc: Return Item property Darkvision.

functions.name: ItemPropertyDecreaseAbility
doc: Return Item property decrease ability score.  You must specify the ability
constant(IP_CONST_ABILITY_*) and the modifier constant.  The modifier must be
a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 10 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyDecreaseAC
doc: Returns Item property decrease Armor Class.  You must specify the armor
modifier type constant(IP_CONST_ACMODIFIERTYPE_*) and the armor class penalty.
The penalty must be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 5 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyDecreaseSkill
doc: Returns Item property decrease skill.  You must specify the constant for the
skill to be decreased(SKILL_*) and the amount of the penalty.  The penalty
must be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 10 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyContainerReducedWeight
doc: Returns Item property container reduced weight.  This is used for special
containers that reduce the weight of the objects inside them.  You must
specify the container weight reduction type constant(IP_CONST_CONTAINERWEIGHTRED_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyExtraMeleeDamageType
doc: Returns Item property extra melee damage type.  You must specify the extra
melee base damage type that you want applied.  It is a constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*).
NOTE: only the first 3 base types (piercing, slashing, & bludgeoning are applicable
here.
NOTE: It is also only applicable to melee weapons.

functions.name: ItemPropertyExtraRangeDamageType
doc: Returns Item property extra ranged damage type.  You must specify the extra
melee base damage type that you want applied.  It is a constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGETYPE_*).
NOTE: only the first 3 base types (piercing, slashing, & bludgeoning are applicable
here.
NOTE: It is also only applicable to ranged weapons.

functions.name: ItemPropertyHaste
doc: Returns Item property haste.

functions.name: ItemPropertyHolyAvenger
doc: Returns Item property Holy Avenger.

functions.name: ItemPropertyImmunityMisc
doc: Returns Item property immunity to miscellaneous effects.  You must specify the
effect to which the user is immune, it is a constant(IP_CONST_IMMUNITYMISC_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyImprovedEvasion
doc: Returns Item property improved evasion.

functions.name: ItemPropertyBonusSpellResistance
doc: Returns Item property bonus spell resistance.  You must specify the bonus spell
resistance constant(IP_CONST_SPELLRESISTANCEBONUS_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyBonusSavingThrowVsX
doc: Returns Item property saving throw bonus vs. a specific effect or damage type.
You must specify the save type constant(IP_CONST_SAVEVS_*) that the bonus is
applied to and the bonus that is be applied.  The bonus must be an integer
between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyBonusSavingThrow
doc: Returns Item property saving throw bonus to the base type (ie. will, reflex,
fortitude).  You must specify the base type constant(IP_CONST_SAVEBASETYPE_*)
to which the user gets the bonus and the bonus that he/she will get.  The
bonus must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyKeen
doc: Returns Item property keen.  This means a critical threat range of 19-20 on a
weapon will be increased to 17-20 etc.

functions.name: ItemPropertyLight
doc: Returns Item property light.  You must specify the intesity constant of the
light(IP_CONST_LIGHTBRIGHTNESS_*) and the color constant of the light
(IP_CONST_LIGHTCOLOR_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyMaxRangeStrengthMod
doc: Returns Item property Max range strength modification (ie. mighty).  You must
specify the maximum modifier for strength that is allowed on a ranged weapon.
The modifier must be a positive integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyNoDamage
doc: Returns Item property no damage.  This means the weapon will do no damage in
combat.

functions.name: ItemPropertyOnHitProps
functions.params: int nProperty, int nSaveDC, int nSpecial = 0
functions.signature: itemproperty ItemPropertyOnHitProps(int nProperty, int nSaveDC, int nSpecial = 0)
doc: Returns Item property on hit -> do effect property.  You must specify the on
hit property constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_*) and the save DC constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_SAVEDC_*).
Some of the item properties require a special parameter as well.  If the
property does not require one you may leave out the last one.  The list of
the ones with 3 parameters and what they are are as follows:
ABILITYDRAIN      :nSpecial is the ability it is to drain.
constant(IP_CONST_ABILITY_*)
BLINDNESS         :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
CONFUSION         :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
DAZE              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
DEAFNESS          :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
DISEASE           :nSpecial is the type of desease that will effect the victim.
constant(DISEASE_*)
DOOM              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
FEAR              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
HOLD              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
ITEMPOISON        :nSpecial is the type of poison that will effect the victim.
constant(IP_CONST_POISON_*)
SILENCE           :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
SLAYRACE          :nSpecial is the race that will be slain.
constant(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*)
SLAYALIGNMENTGROUP:nSpecial is the alignment group that will be slain(ie. chaotic).
constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*)
SLAYALIGNMENT     :nSpecial is the specific alignment that will be slain.
constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*)
SLEEP             :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
SLOW              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)
STUN              :nSpecial is the duration/percentage of effecting victim.
constant(IP_CONST_ONHIT_DURATION_*)

functions.name: ItemPropertyReducedSavingThrowVsX
doc: Returns Item property reduced saving throw vs. an effect or damage type.  You must
specify the constant to which the penalty applies(IP_CONST_SAVEVS_*) and the
penalty to be applied.  The penalty must be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 20

functions.name: ItemPropertyReducedSavingThrow
doc: Returns Item property reduced saving to base type.  You must specify the base
type to which the penalty applies (ie. will, reflex, or fortitude) and the penalty
to be applied.  The constant for the base type starts with (IP_CONST_SAVEBASETYPE_*).
The penalty must be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 20 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyRegeneration
doc: Returns Item property regeneration.  You must specify the regeneration amount.
The amount must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertySkillBonus
doc: Returns Item property skill bonus.  You must specify the skill to which the user
will get a bonus(SKILL_*) and the amount of the bonus.  The bonus amount must
be an integer between 1 and 50.

functions.name: ItemPropertySpellImmunitySpecific
doc: Returns Item property spell immunity vs. specific spell.  You must specify the
spell to which the user will be immune(IP_CONST_IMMUNITYSPELL_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertySpellImmunitySchool
doc: Returns Item property spell immunity vs. spell school.  You must specify the
school to which the user will be immune(IP_CONST_SPELLSCHOOL_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyThievesTools
doc: Returns Item property Thieves tools.  You must specify the modifier you wish
the tools to have.  The modifier must be an integer between 1 and 12.

functions.name: ItemPropertyAttackBonus
doc: Returns Item property Attack bonus.  You must specify an attack bonus.  The bonus
must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyAttackBonusVsAlign
doc: Returns Item property Attack bonus vs. alignment group.  You must specify the
alignment group constant(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*) and the attack bonus.  The
bonus must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyAttackBonusVsRace
doc: Returns Item property attack bonus vs. racial group.  You must specify the
racial group constant(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*) and the attack bonus.  The bonus
must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyAttackBonusVsSAlign
doc: Returns Item property attack bonus vs. a specific alignment.  You must specify
the alignment you want the bonus to work against(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*) and the
attack bonus.  The bonus must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyAttackPenalty
doc: Returns Item property attack penalty.  You must specify the attack penalty.
The penalty must be a POSITIVE integer between 1 and 5 (ie. 1 = -1).

functions.name: ItemPropertyUnlimitedAmmo
doc: Returns Item property unlimited ammo.  If you leave the parameter field blank
it will be just a normal bolt, arrow, or bullet.  However you may specify that
you want the ammunition to do special damage (ie. +1d6 Fire, or +1 enhancement
bonus).  For this parmeter you use the constants beginning with:
(IP_CONST_UNLIMITEDAMMO_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyLimitUseByAlign
doc: Returns Item property limit use by alignment group.  You must specify the
alignment group(s) that you want to be able to use this item(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENTGROUP_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyLimitUseByClass
doc: Returns Item property limit use by class.  You must specify the class(es) who
are able to use this item(IP_CONST_CLASS_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyLimitUseByRace
doc: Returns Item property limit use by race.  You must specify the race(s) who are
allowed to use this item(IP_CONST_RACIALTYPE_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyLimitUseBySAlign
doc: Returns Item property limit use by specific alignment.  You must specify the
alignment(s) of those allowed to use the item(IP_CONST_ALIGNMENT_*).

functions.name: BadBadReplaceMeThisDoesNothing
doc: replace this function it does nothing.

functions.name: ItemPropertyVampiricRegeneration
doc: Returns Item property vampiric regeneration.  You must specify the amount of
regeneration.  The regen amount must be an integer between 1 and 20.

functions.name: ItemPropertyTrap
doc: Returns Item property Trap.  You must specify the trap level constant
(IP_CONST_TRAPSTRENGTH_*) and the trap type constant(IP_CONST_TRAPTYPE_*).

functions.name: ItemPropertyTrueSeeing
doc: Returns Item property true seeing.

functions.name: ItemPropertyOnMonsterHitProperties
doc: Returns Item property Monster on hit apply effect property.  You must specify
the property that you want applied on hit.  There are some properties that
require an additional special parameter to be specified.  The others that
don't require any additional parameter you may just put in the one.  The
special cases are as follows:
ABILITYDRAIN:nSpecial is the ability to drain.
constant(IP_CONST_ABILITY_*)
DISEASE     :nSpecial is the disease that you want applied.
constant(DISEASE_*)
LEVELDRAIN  :nSpecial is the number of levels that you want drained.
integer between 1 and 5.
POISON      :nSpecial is the type of poison that will effect the victim.
constant(IP_CONST_POISON_*)
WOUNDING    :nSpecial is the amount of wounding.
integer between 1 and 5.
NOTE: Any that do not appear in the above list do not require the second
parameter.
NOTE: These can only be applied to monster NATURAL weapons (ie. bite, claw,
gore, and slam).  IT WILL NOT WORK ON NORMAL WEAPONS.

functions.name: ItemPropertyTurnResistance
doc: Returns Item property turn resistance.  You must specify the resistance bonus.
The bonus must be an integer between 1 and 50.

functions.name: ItemPropertyMassiveCritical
doc: Returns Item property Massive Critical.  You must specify the extra damage
constant(IP_CONST_DAMAGEBONUS_*) of the criticals.

functions.name: ItemPropertyFreeAction
doc: Returns Item property free action.

functions.name: ItemPropertyMonsterDamage
doc: Returns Item property monster damage.  You must specify the amount of damage
the monster's attack will do(IP_CONST_MONSTERDAMAGE_*).
NOTE: These can only be applied to monster NATURAL weapons (ie. bite, claw,
gore, and slam).  IT WILL NOT WORK ON NORMAL WEAPONS.

functions.name: ItemPropertyImmunityToSpellLevel
doc: Returns Item property immunity to spell level.  You must specify the level of
which that and below the user will be immune.  The level must be an integer
between 1 and 9.  By putting in a 3 it will mean the user is immune to all
3rd level and lower spells.

functions.name: ItemPropertySpecialWalk
doc: Returns Item property special walk.  If no parameters are specified it will
automatically use the zombie walk.  This will apply the special walk animation
to the user.

functions.name: ItemPropertyHealersKit
doc: Returns Item property healers kit.  You must specify the level of the kit.
The modifier must be an integer between 1 and 12.

functions.name: ItemPropertyWeightIncrease
doc: Returns Item property weight increase.  You must specify the weight increase
constant(IP_CONST_WEIGHTINCREASE_*).

functions.name: GetIsSkillSuccessful
functions.params: object oTarget, int nSkill, int nDifficulty
functions.signature: int GetIsSkillSuccessful(object oTarget, int nSkill, int nDifficulty)
doc: Returns true if 1d20 roll + skill rank is greater than or equal to difficulty
- oTarget: the creature using the skill
- nSkill: the skill being used
- nDifficulty: Difficulty class of skill

functions.name: EffectSpellFailure
functions.params: int nPercent = 100, int nSpellSchool = SPELL_SCHOOL_GENERAL, int nSpellFailureType = SPELL_FAILURE_TYPE_ALL
functions.signature: effect EffectSpellFailure(int nPercent = 100, int nSpellSchool = SPELL_SCHOOL_GENERAL, int nSpellFailureType = SPELL_FAILURE_TYPE_ALL)
doc: Creates an effect that inhibits spells
- nPercent - percentage of failure
- nSpellSchool - the school of spells affected. Only applies to SPELL_FAILURE_TYPE_ALL.
- nSpellFailureType - Use SPELL_FAILURE_TYPE_* constants for different spell failure types

functions.name: SpeakStringByStrRef
functions.signature: void SpeakStringByStrRef(int nStrRef, int nTalkVolume = TALKVOLUME_TALK)
doc: Causes the object to instantly speak a translated string.
(not an action, not blocked when uncommandable)
- nStrRef: Reference of the string in the talk table
- nTalkVolume: TALKVOLUME_*

functions.name: SetCutsceneMode
functions.params: object oCreature, int nInCutscene = TRUE, int nLeftClickingEnabled = FALSE
functions.signature: void SetCutsceneMode(object oCreature, int nInCutscene = TRUE, int nLeftClickingEnabled = FALSE)
doc: Sets the given creature into cutscene mode.  This prevents the player from
using the GUI and camera controls.
- oCreature: creature in a cutscene
- nInCutscene: TRUE to move them into cutscene, FALSE to remove cutscene mode
- nLeftClickingEnabled: TRUE to allow the user to interact with the game world using the left mouse button only.
FALSE to stop the user from interacting with the game world.
Note: SetCutsceneMode(oPlayer, TRUE) will also make the player 'plot' (unkillable).
SetCutsceneMode(oPlayer, FALSE) will restore the player's plot flag to what it
was when SetCutsceneMode(oPlayer, TRUE) was called.

functions.name: GetLastPCToCancelCutscene
doc: Gets the last player character to cancel from a cutscene.

functions.name: GetDialogSoundLength
doc: Gets the length of the specified wavefile, in seconds
Only works for sounds used for dialog.

functions.name: FadeFromBlack
functions.signature: void FadeFromBlack(object oCreature, float fSpeed = FADE_SPEED_MEDIUM)
doc: Fades the screen for the given creature/player from black to regular screen
- oCreature: creature controlled by player that should fade from black

functions.name: FadeToBlack
functions.signature: void FadeToBlack(object oCreature, float fSpeed = FADE_SPEED_MEDIUM)
doc: Fades the screen for the given creature/player from regular screen to black
- oCreature: creature controlled by player that should fade to black

functions.name: StopFade
doc: Removes any fading or black screen.
- oCreature: creature controlled by player that should be cleared

functions.name: BlackScreen
doc: Sets the screen to black.  Can be used in preparation for a fade-in (FadeFromBlack)
Can be cleared by either doing a FadeFromBlack, or by calling StopFade.
- oCreature: creature controlled by player that should see black screen

functions.name: GetBaseAttackBonus
doc: Returns the base attach bonus for the given creature.

functions.name: SetImmortal
doc: Set a creature's immortality flag.
-oCreature: creature affected
-bImmortal: TRUE = creature is immortal and cannot be killed (but still takes damage)
FALSE = creature is not immortal and is damaged normally.
This scripting command only works on Creature objects.

functions.name: OpenInventory
doc: Open's this creature's inventory panel for this player
- oCreature: creature to view
- oPlayer: the owner of this creature will see the panel pop up
DM's can view any creature's inventory
Players can view their own inventory, or that of their henchman, familiar or animal companion

functions.name: StoreCameraFacing
doc: Stores the current camera mode and position so that it can be restored (using
RestoreCameraFacing())

functions.name: RestoreCameraFacing
doc: Restores the camera mode and position to what they were last time StoreCameraFacing
was called.  RestoreCameraFacing can only be called once, and must correspond to a
previous call to StoreCameraFacing.

functions.name: LevelUpHenchman
functions.params: object oCreature, int nClass = CLASS_TYPE_INVALID, int bReadyAllSpells = FALSE, int nPackage = PACKAGE_INVALID
functions.signature: int LevelUpHenchman(object oCreature, int nClass = CLASS_TYPE_INVALID, int bReadyAllSpells = FALSE, int nPackage = PACKAGE_INVALID)
doc: Levels up a creature using default settings.
If successfull it returns the level the creature now is, or 0 if it fails.
If you want to give them a different level (ie: Give a Fighter a level of Wizard)
you can specify that in the nClass.
However, if you specify a class to which the creature no package specified,
they will use the default package for that class for their levelup choices.
(ie: no Barbarian Savage/Wizard Divination combinations)
If you turn on bReadyAllSpells, all memorized spells will be ready to cast without resting.
if nPackage is PACKAGE_INVALID then it will use the starting package assigned to that class or just the class package

functions.name: SetDroppableFlag
doc: Sets the droppable flag on an item
- oItem: the item to change
- bDroppable: TRUE or FALSE, whether the item should be droppable
Droppable items will appear on a creature's remains when the creature is killed.

functions.name: GetWeight
doc: Gets the weight of an item, or the total carried weight of a creature in tenths
of pounds (as per the baseitems.2da).
- oTarget: the item or creature for which the weight is needed

functions.name: GetModuleItemAcquiredBy
doc: Gets the object that acquired the module item.  May be a creature, item, or placeable

functions.name: GetImmortal
doc: Get the immortal flag on a creature

functions.name: DoWhirlwindAttack
functions.params: int bDisplayFeedback = TRUE, int bImproved = FALSE
functions.signature: void DoWhirlwindAttack(int bDisplayFeedback = TRUE, int bImproved = FALSE)
doc: Does a single attack on every hostile creature within 10ft. of the attacker
and determines damage accordingly.  If the attacker has a ranged weapon
equipped, this will have no effect.
NOTE ** This is meant to be called inside the spell script for whirlwind
attack, it is not meant to be used to queue up a new whirlwind attack.  To do
that you need to call ActionUseFeat(FEAT_WHIRLWIND_ATTACK, oEnemy)
- int bDisplayFeedback: TRUE or FALSE, whether or not feedback should be
displayed
- int bImproved: If TRUE, the improved version of whirlwind is used

functions.name: Get2DAString
functions.params: string s2DA, string sColumn, int nRow
functions.signature: string Get2DAString(string s2DA, string sColumn, int nRow)
doc: Gets a value from a 2DA file on the server and returns it as a string
avoid using this function in loops
- s2DA: the name of the 2da file, 16 chars max
- sColumn: the name of the column in the 2da
- nRow: the row in the 2da
returns an empty string if file, row, or column not found

functions.name: EffectEthereal
doc: Returns an effect of type EFFECT_TYPE_ETHEREAL which works just like EffectSanctuary
except that the observers get no saving throw

functions.name: GetAILevel
doc: Gets the current AI Level that the creature is running at.
Returns one of the following:
AI_LEVEL_INVALID, AI_LEVEL_VERY_LOW, AI_LEVEL_LOW, AI_LEVEL_NORMAL, AI_LEVEL_HIGH, AI_LEVEL_VERY_HIGH

functions.name: SetAILevel
doc: Sets the current AI Level of the creature to the value specified. Does not work on Players.
The game by default will choose an appropriate AI level for
creatures based on the circumstances that the creature is in.
Explicitly setting an AI level will over ride the game AI settings.
The new setting will last until SetAILevel is called again with the argument AI_LEVEL_DEFAULT.
AI_LEVEL_DEFAULT  - Default setting. The game will take over seting the appropriate AI level when required.
AI_LEVEL_VERY_LOW - Very Low priority, very stupid, but low CPU usage for AI. Typically used when no players are in the area.
AI_LEVEL_LOW      - Low priority, mildly stupid, but slightly more CPU usage for AI. Typically used when not in combat, but a player is in the area.
AI_LEVEL_NORMAL   - Normal priority, average AI, but more CPU usage required for AI. Typically used when creature is in combat.
AI_LEVEL_HIGH     - High priority, smartest AI, but extremely high CPU usage required for AI. Avoid using this. It is most likely only ever needed for cutscenes.

functions.name: GetIsPossessedFamiliar
doc: This will return TRUE if the creature running the script is a familiar currently
possessed by his master.
returns FALSE if not or if the creature object is invalid

functions.name: UnpossessFamiliar
doc: This will cause a Player Creature to unpossess his/her familiar.  It will work if run
on the player creature or the possessed familiar.  It does not work in conjunction with
any DM possession.

functions.name: GetIsAreaInterior
doc: This will return TRUE if the area is flagged as either interior or underground.

functions.name: SendMessageToPCByStrRef
doc: Send a server message (szMessage) to the oPlayer.

functions.name: IncrementRemainingFeatUses
doc: Increment the remaining uses per day for this creature by one.
Total number of feats per day can not exceed the maximum.
- oCreature: creature to modify
- nFeat: constant FEAT_*

functions.name: ExportSingleCharacter
doc: Force the character of the player specified to be exported to its respective directory
i.e. LocalVault/ServerVault/ etc.

functions.name: PlaySoundByStrRef
functions.signature: void PlaySoundByStrRef(int nStrRef, int nRunAsAction = TRUE)
doc: This will play a sound that is associated with a stringRef, it will be a mono sound from the location of the object running the command.
if nRunAsAction is off then the sound is forced to play intantly.

functions.name: SetSubRace
doc: Set the name of oCreature's sub race to sSubRace.

functions.name: SetDeity
doc: Set the name of oCreature's Deity to sDeity.

functions.name: GetIsDMPossessed
doc: Returns TRUE if the creature oCreature is currently possessed by a DM character.
Returns FALSE otherwise.
Note: GetIsDMPossessed() will return FALSE if oCreature is the DM character.
To determine if oCreature is a DM character use GetIsDM()

functions.name: GetWeather
doc: Gets the current weather conditions for the area oArea.
Returns: WEATHER_CLEAR, WEATHER_RAIN, WEATHER_SNOW, WEATHER_INVALID
Note: If called on an Interior area, this will always return WEATHER_CLEAR.

functions.name: GetIsAreaNatural
doc: Returns AREA_NATURAL if the area oArea is natural, AREA_ARTIFICIAL otherwise.
Returns AREA_INVALID, on an error.

functions.name: GetIsAreaAboveGround
doc: Returns AREA_ABOVEGROUND if the area oArea is above ground, AREA_UNDERGROUND otherwise.
Returns AREA_INVALID, on an error.

functions.name: GetPCItemLastEquipped
doc: Use this to get the item last equipped by a player character in OnPlayerEquipItem..

functions.name: GetPCItemLastEquippedBy
doc: Use this to get the player character who last equipped an item in OnPlayerEquipItem..

functions.name: GetPCItemLastUnequipped
doc: Use this to get the item last unequipped by a player character in OnPlayerEquipItem..

functions.name: GetPCItemLastUnequippedBy
doc: Use this to get the player character who last unequipped an item in OnPlayerUnEquipItem..

functions.name: CopyItemAndModify
functions.params: object oItem, int nType, int nIndex, int nNewValue, int bCopyVars = FALSE
functions.signature: object CopyItemAndModify(object oItem, int nType, int nIndex, int nNewValue, int bCopyVars = FALSE)
doc: Creates a new copy of an item, while making a single change to the appearance of the item.
Helmet models and simple items ignore iIndex.
iType                            iIndex                              iNewValue
ITEM_APPR_TYPE_SIMPLE_MODEL      [Ignored]                           Model #
ITEM_APPR_TYPE_WEAPON_COLOR      ITEM_APPR_WEAPON_COLOR_*            1-4
ITEM_APPR_TYPE_WEAPON_MODEL      ITEM_APPR_WEAPON_MODEL_*            Model #
ITEM_APPR_TYPE_ARMOR_MODEL       ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_MODEL_*             Model #
ITEM_APPR_TYPE_ARMOR_COLOR       ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_COLOR_* [0]         0-175 [1]
[0] Alternatively, where ITEM_APPR_TYPE_ARMOR_COLOR is specified, if per-part coloring is
desired, the following equation can be used for nIndex to achieve that:
ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_NUM_COLORS + (ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_MODEL_ * ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_NUM_COLORS) + ITEM_APPR_ARMOR_COLOR_
For example, to change the CLOTH1 channel of the torso, nIndex would be:
[1] When specifying per-part coloring, the value 255 is allowed and corresponds with the logical
function 'clear colour override', which clears the per-part override for that part.

functions.name: GetItemAppearance
functions.params: object oItem, int nType, int nIndex
functions.signature: int GetItemAppearance(object oItem, int nType, int nIndex)
doc: Queries the current value of the appearance settings on an item. The parameters are
identical to those of CopyItemAndModify().

functions.name: ItemPropertyOnHitCastSpell
doc: Creates an item property that (when applied to a weapon item) causes a spell to be cast
when a successful strike is made, or (when applied to armor) is struck by an opponent.
- nSpell uses the IP_CONST_ONHIT_CASTSPELL_* constants

functions.name: GetItemPropertySubType
doc: Returns the SubType number of the item property. See the 2DA files for value definitions.

functions.name: GetActionMode
doc: Gets the status of ACTION_MODE_* modes on a creature.

functions.name: SetActionMode
functions.params: object oCreature, int nMode, int nStatus
functions.signature: void SetActionMode(object oCreature, int nMode, int nStatus)
doc: Sets the status of modes ACTION_MODE_* on a creature.

functions.name: GetArcaneSpellFailure
doc: Returns the current arcane spell failure factor of a creature

functions.name: ActionExamine
doc: Makes a player examine the object oExamine. This causes the examination
pop-up box to appear for the object specified.

functions.name: ItemPropertyVisualEffect
doc: Creates a visual effect (ITEM_VISUAL_*) that may be applied to
melee weapons only.

functions.name: SetLootable
doc: Sets the lootable state of a *living* NPC creature.
This function will *not* work on players or dead creatures.

functions.name: GetLootable
doc: Returns the lootable state of a creature.

functions.name: GetCutsceneCameraMoveRate
doc: Returns the current movement rate factor
of the cutscene 'camera man'.
NOTE: This will be a value between 0.1, 2.0 (10%-200%)

functions.name: SetCutsceneCameraMoveRate
doc: Sets the current movement rate factor for the cutscene
camera man.
NOTE: You can only set values between 0.1, 2.0 (10%-200%)

functions.name: GetItemCursedFlag
doc: Returns TRUE if the item is cursed and cannot be dropped

functions.name: SetItemCursedFlag
doc: When cursed, items cannot be dropped

functions.name: SetMaxHenchmen
doc: Sets the maximum number of henchmen

functions.name: GetMaxHenchmen
