Called from all the Play_* functions.

functions.name: PlayMobileAmbientAnimations_NonAvian
doc: This function should be used for mobile NPCs and monsters
other than avian ones. It should be called by the creature
that you want to perform the animations.
Creatures will move randomly between objects in their
area that have the tag "NW_STOP". Injured creatures will
go to the nearest "NW_SAFE" waypoint and rest there.
If at any point a creature gets to an "NW_DETECT" or
"NW_STEALTH" waypoint, they will toggle on/off detect or
stealth mode as appropriate.
Humanoid creatures will also perform the following actions
(you can set the NW_ANIM_FLAG_IS_CIVILIZED flag in script
on non-humanoid creatures to make them use these behaviors):
Creatures who are spawned in an area with the "NW_HOME" tag
will mark that area as their home, leave during the day,
and return at night.
Creatures who are spawned in an outdoor area (for instance,
in city streets) will go inside areas that have one of the
interior waypoints (NW_TAVERN, NW_SHOP), if those areas
are connected by an unlocked door. They will come back out
as well.
Mobile creatures will also have all the behaviors of immobile
creatures, just tending to move around more.

functions.name: PlayMobileAmbientAnimations_Avian
doc: Avian creatures will fly around randomly.

functions.name: PlayImmobileAmbientAnimations
doc: This function should be used for any NPCs that should
not move around. It should be called by the creature
that you want to perform the animations.
Creatures who call this function will never leave the
area they spawned in.
Injured creatures will rest at their starting location.
Creatures who have the NW_ANIM_FLAG_IS_MOBILE_CLOSE_RANGE
flag set will move around slightly within the area.
Creatures in an area with an "interior" waypoint (NW_HOME,
NW_SHOP, NW_TAVERN) will be set to have this flag automatically.
Close-range creatures will move around the area, frequently
returning to their starting point, interacting with other
creatures and placeables. They will visit NW_STOP waypoints
in their immediate vicinity, and they will close opened doors.
In all other cases, the creature will not move from its starting
position. They will turn around randomly, turn to and 'talk' to
other NPCs in their immediate vicinity, and interact with
placeables in their immediate vicinity.

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomImmobile
doc: Perform a strictly immobile action.
Includes:
- play a random animation
- turn towards a nearby unoccupied friend and 'talk'
- turn towards a nearby placeable and interact
- turn around randomly

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomCloseRange
doc: Perform a random close-range action.
This will include:
- go to a nearby placeable and interact with it
- go to a nearby friend and interact with them
- play a random animation
- walk to a nearby 'NW_STOP' waypoint
- close an open door and return
- go back to starting position
- fall through to ActionPlayRandomImmobile

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomMobile
doc: Perform a mobile action.
Includes:
- walk to an 'NW_STOP' waypoint in the area
- walk to an area door and possibly go inside
- go outside if previously went inside

functions.name: PlayMobileAmbientAnimations_NonAvian
doc: This function should be used for mobile NPCs and monsters
other than avian ones. It should be called by the creature
that you want to perform the animations.
Creatures will interact with each other and move around,
possibly even moving between areas.
Creatures who are spawned in an area with the "NW_HOME" tag
will mark that area as their home, leave from the nearest
door during the day, and return at night.
Injured creatures will go to the nearest "NW_SAFE" waypoint
in their immediate area and rest there.
If at any point the nearest waypoint is "NW_DETECT" or
"NW_STEALTH", the creature will toggle search/stealth mode
respectively.
Creatures who are spawned in an outdoor area (for instance,
in city streets) will go inside areas that have one of the
interior waypoints (NW_TAVERN, NW_SHOP), if those areas
are connected by an unlocked door. They will come back out
as well.
Creatures will also move randomly between objects in their
area that have the tag "NW_STOP".
Mobile creatures will have all the same behaviors as immobile
creatures, just tending to move around more.

functions.name: PlayMobileAmbientAnimations_Avian
doc: Avian creatures will fly around randomly.

functions.name: PlayImmobileAmbientAnimations
doc: This function should be used for any NPCs that should
not move around. It should be called by the creature
that you want to perform the animations.
Creatures who call this function will never leave the
area they spawned in.
Injured creatures will rest at their starting location.
Creatures who have the NW_ANIM_FLAG_IS_MOBILE_CLOSE_RANGE
flag set will move around slightly within the area.
Creatures in an area with an "interior" waypoint (NW_HOME,
NW_SHOP, NW_TAVERN) will be set to have this flag automatically.
Close-range creatures will move around the area, frequently
returning to their starting point, interacting with other
creatures and placeables. They will visit NW_STOP waypoints
in their immediate vicinity, and they will close opened doors.
In all other cases, the creature will not move from its starting
position. They will turn around randomly, turn to and 'talk' to
other NPCs in their immediate vicinity, and interact with
placeables in their immediate vicinity.

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomImmobile

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomCloseRange
doc: Perform a random close-range action.
This will include:
- any of the immobile actions
- close any nearby doors, then return to current position
- go to a nearby placeable and interact with it
- go to a nearby friend and interact with them
- walk to a nearby 'NW_STOP' waypoint
- going back to starting point

functions.name: AnimActionPlayRandomMobile
doc: Perform a mobile action.
Includes:
- walk to an 'NW_STOP' waypoint in the area
- walk to an area door and possibly go inside
- go outside if previously went inside
- fall through to AnimActionPlayRandomCloseRange

functions.name: SpecialTactics
doc: This function checks for the special tactics flags and
chooses tactics appropriately for each.
Currently available:
X0_COMBAT_FLAG_{RANGED,DEFENSIVE,COWARDLY,AMBUSHER}
Note that only one special tactics flag should be applied
to a single creature for the most part!
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsRanged
doc: Special tactics for ranged fighters.
The caller will attempt to stay in ranged distance and
will make use of active ranged combat feats (Rapid Shot
and Called Shot).
If the target is too close and is not currently attacking
the caller, the caller will instead try to find a ranged
enemy to attack. If that fails, the caller will try to run
away from the target to a ranged distance.
This will fall through and return FALSE after three
consecutive attempts to get away from an opponent within
melee distance, at which point the caller will use normal
tactics until they are again at a ranged distance from
their target.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsAmbusher
doc: Special tactics for ambushers.
Ambushers will first attempt to get out of sight
of their target if currently visible to that target.
If not visible to the target, they will use any invisibility/
hide powers they have.
Once hidden, they will then attempt to attack the target using
standard AI.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsCowardly
doc: Special tactics for cowardly creatures
Cowards act as follows:
- if you and your friends outnumber the enemy by 6:1 or
by more than 10, fall through to normal combat.
- if you are currently being attacked by a melee attacker,
fight defensively (see SpecialTacticsDefensive).
- if there is a "NW_SAFE" waypoint in your area that is
out of sight of the target, run to it.
- otherwise, run away randomly from the target.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsDefensive
doc: Special tactics for defensive fighters
This will attempt to use the active defensive feats such as
Knockdown and Expertise, and also use Parry mode, when these
are appropriate. Falls through to standard combat on failure.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: GetCombatCondition

doc: Determine whether the specified X0_COMBAT_FLAG_* is set on the target

standard AI.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsCowardly
doc: Special tactics for cowardly creatures
Cowards act as follows:
- if you and your friends outnumber the enemy by 6:1 or
by more than 10, fall through to normal combat.
- if you are currently being attacked by a melee attacker,
fight defensively (see SpecialTacticsDefensive).
- if there is a "NW_SAFE" waypoint in your area that is
out of sight of the target, run to it.
- otherwise, run away randomly from the target.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: SpecialTacticsDefensive
doc: Special tactics for defensive fighters
This will attempt to use the active defensive feats such as
Knockdown and Expertise, and also use Parry mode, when these
are appropriate. Falls through to standard combat on failure.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE on failure.

functions.name: GetChapter
doc: THIS WILL ONLY WORK CORRECTLY IN EXPANSION PACK 0.

Module 2: 2
Module 3: 3

functions.name: EndModule

no end modules for XP1 now.

functions.params: object oVictim, object oCorpse, int bDecay = TRUE, int bDropWielded = TRUE
functions.signature: void LootInventorySlots(object oVictim, object oCorpse, int bDecay = TRUE, int bDropWielded = TRUE)
doc: Loot all the droppable items from the inventory slots of the
victim into the inventory of the corpse object.
If bDropWielded is TRUE, the items the victim is wielding in
its right and left hands will be dropped to either side
of it, otherwise they will simply be placed into inventory.

functions.name: LootInventory
doc: Strip everything droppable in the regular inventory.
Use LootInventorySlots to strip equipped items.

functions.name: KillAndReplaceLootable
functions.params: object oVictim, int bDecay = TRUE, int bDropWielded = TRUE
functions.signature: void KillAndReplaceLootable(object oVictim, int bDecay = TRUE, int bDropWielded = TRUE)
doc: Die young, leave a lootable corpse. (ha ha ha... okay, been working too long)
Despite the name, can be used in an OnDeath script;
it won't kill the victim twice.
If bDecay is TRUE, the corpse will decay to a bag after a brief delay.
If bDropWielded is TRUE, the corpse will drop its right/left hand items
to either side (making the body more decorative, but the items harder
to see).

functions.name: KillAndReplaceDecay
doc: Kill and leave a decorative corpse that will decay
after a short while.
Despite the name, can be used in an OnDeath script;
it won't kill the victim twice.

functions.name: KillAndReplaceRaiseable
doc: Kill and leave a raiseable & selectable corpse
Despite the name, can be used in an OnDeath script;
it won't kill the victim twice.

functions.name: KillAndReplaceSelectable
doc: Kill and leave a corpse with the corpse's name
Despite the name, can be used in an OnDeath script;
it won't kill the victim twice.

functions.name: KillAndReplaceDecorative
doc: Kill and leave a purely decorative corpse (no name,
not raiseable).
Despite the name, can be used in an OnDeath script;
it won't kill the victim twice.

functions.signature: void KillAndExplode(object oVictim, int nSpell = SPELL_FIREBALL)

doc: Campaign variable table database control.
Use the set functions to add/change a variable to a campaign database.
Use the get functions to retrieve the variable from a campaign database
Use the delete functions to remove the variable from a campaign database.
The database name must be the same for both set and get functions. It IS case sensitive.
The var name must be unique acrossed the entire database.
If you want a variable to pertain to a specific player in the game, provide a player object.

functions.name: GetSpecialAreaForDeckCard
doc: Get a special deck-specific area if it exists and is available.
This will start by checking for an area with just the given area
tag. If that doesn't exist or is taken, it will start looking
for areas tagged sAreaTag + number, until it finds one that is available.
X0_DECK_DONJON1, X0_DECK_DONJON2, etc
Returns OBJECT_INVALID if no area found.

functions.name: DoSpecialAreaDeckCard
functions.params: object oCaster, string sAreaTag, int nTurn = 0
functions.signature: int DoSpecialAreaDeckCard(object oCaster, string sAreaTag, int nTurn = 0)
doc: Get a special area and transport the caster (with
associates but without party members) to the
X0_DECK_START waypoint inside it.
Returns TRUE on success, FALSE otherwise.

functions.name: DoFoolDeckCard
doc: The Fool card (negative)
Lose 10,000 XP and get 2 extra cards.

functions.name: DoDonjonDeckCard
doc: The Donjon card (negative)
If a donjon area is available, the caster and associates are transported
there.
The area should be tagged X0_DECK_DONJON1.
The starting location should be tagged X0_DECK_START.
See the Gauntlet area in module 3 of XP1 as an example.
(This is a good model to use if you want to create a "Keep" or
"Void" type card for your own module.)

functions.name: DoTraitorDeckCard
doc: The Traitor card (negative)
Change to diametrically-opposed alignment instantly.

functions.name: DoKnaveDeckCard
doc: The Knave card (negative)
All non-plot possessions not currently equipped are destroyed.

functions.name: DoPlagueDeckCard
doc: The Plague card (negative)
Permanent disease effect applied for duration of the module.

functions.name: DoLookingGlassDeckCard
doc: The Looking Glass card (negative)
Caster's henchman is replaced with an evil doppelganger.

functions.name: DoWyrmDeckCard
doc: The Wyrm card (negative)
A hostile ancient dragon is lured to the caster.

functions.name: DoJokerDeckCard
doc: The Joker card (positive)
Gain 10,000 XP and get 2 extra cards.

functions.name: DoHoardDeckCard
doc: The Hoard card (positive)
Gain 50-100,000 gold instantly

functions.name: DoOracleDeckCard
doc: The Oracle card (positive)
Gain permanent premonition effect for duration of the module

functions.name: DoAvatarDeckCard
doc: The Avatar card (positive)
Caster is transformed into an avatar of their alignment.

functions.name: DoFountainDeckCard
doc: The Fountain card (positive)
All of caster's items are recharged and all stacked items are
filled up to the maximum possible number.

functions.name: DoHatchlingDeckCard
doc: The Hatchling card (positive)
A wyrmling of alignment-appropriate color appears and follows
the caster as a summoned creature until damaged, at which point
it transforms into an adult dragon and fights on the caster's behalf
until all enemies are slain, then vanishes.

functions.name: DoBequestDeckCard
doc: The Bequest card (positive)
Gain a major unique magical item (this will respect the
XP1 treasure system if it is used in the module, otherwise
random boss-level treasure will be generated).

functions.name: GetNearestEnemy
functions.signature: object GetNearestEnemy(object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1)
doc: Gets the nearest enemy.

functions.name: GetNearestPerceivedEnemy
functions.params: object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1, int nCriteriaType = 1000, int nCriteriaValue = 1000
functions.signature: object GetNearestPerceivedEnemy(object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1, int nCriteriaType = 1000, int nCriteriaValue = 1000)
doc: Returns the nearest object that can be seen, then checks for
the nearest heard target.
You may pass in any of the CREATURE_TYPE_* constants
used in GetNearestCreature as nCriteriaType, with
corresponding values for nCriteriaValue, to get a more
specific result.
KLUDGE: Huge default values used here for nCriteriaType/Value
because -1 cannot be passed in at the moment due to a
compiler bug. Should be changed to -1 once that's fixed.

functions.name: GetNearestSeenEnemy
functions.signature: object GetNearestSeenEnemy(object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1)
doc: Get the nearest seen enemy. This will NOT return an enemy that is
heard but not seen; for that, use GetNearestPerceivedEnemy instead.

functions.name: GetNearestSeenOrHeardEnemy
functions.signature: object GetNearestSeenOrHeardEnemy(object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1)
doc: Returns the nearest object that can be seen, then checks for
the nearest heard target.
Now just a wrapper around GetNearestPerceivedEnemy.

functions.name: GetNearestSeenFriend
functions.signature: object GetNearestSeenFriend(object oSource = OBJECT_SELF, int nNth = 1)
doc: Get the nearest seen friend.

functions.name: CountEnemiesAndAllies
functions.signature: struct sSituation CountEnemiesAndAllies(float fRadius = 20.0, object oSource = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Count the number of enemies and allies in a given radius
and their respective total CRs (slightly rounded, since
we use integers instead of floats).
This returns a "struct sSituation" type value. To use, do
something like the following:
struct sSituation sitCurr = CountEnemiesAndAllies(20.0);
int nNumEnemies = sitCurr.ENEMY_NUM;
int nNumAllies = sitCurr.ALLY_NUM;
int nAllyCR = sitCurr.ALLY_CR;
int nEnemyCR = sitCurr.ENEMY_CR;

functions.name: DetermineEnemies
doc: Categorizes the enemies the creature is facing

functions.name: StoreHenchmanItems
doc: Store all items in the henchman's inventory in the campaign DB,
skipping those items which have the henchman's tag in their
name.
This is paired with RetrieveHenchmanItems for the leveling-up
process.

functions.name: RetrieveHenchmanItems
doc: Retrieve (and then delete) all henchman inventory items out of
the campaign DB, putting them in the inventory of the henchman.
This is paired with StoreHenchmanItems for the leveling-up
process.

functions.name: AddHighlight
functions.signature: void AddHighlight(object oTarget, int nVisualEffect = VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE)
doc: Apply a highlight effect to an object. Lasts permanently until
removed.
A few good ones, although any VFX_DUR_ effect can be used:
VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE: glowing, purple, transparent
VFX_DUR_GHOSTLY_VISAGE: glowing, blue, transparent
VFX_DUR_BLUR: alternating blue/white pulsing glow
VFX_DUR_PARALYZED: alternating red/white pulsing glow
VFX_DUR_PROT_SHADOW_ARMOR: shimmery opaque black glow
VFX_DUR_MIND_AFFECTING_DOMINATED: spinning crown of lights
VFX_DUR_MIND_AFFECTING_DISABLED: crown of sparkling lights

functions.name: AddTemporaryHighlight
functions.params: object oTarget, int nVisualEffect = VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE, float fDuration = 10.0f
functions.signature: void AddTemporaryHighlight(object oTarget, int nVisualEffect = VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE, float fDuration = 10.0f)
doc: Adds a temporary highlight to an object.
See comments to AddHighlight for suggested highlight effects.

functions.name: RemoveHighlight
functions.signature: void RemoveHighlight(object oTarget, int nVisualEffect = VFX_NONE)
doc: Remove the highlight from an object.
If not specified, removes the last-added highlight, although
this default behavior will NOT work twice in a row to remove
multiple highlights.

functions.name: TriggerAddHighlight
functions.params: int nVisualEffect = VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE, int nDurationType = DURATION_TYPE_TEMPORARY, float fDuration = 10.0f
functions.signature: void TriggerAddHighlight(int nVisualEffect = VFX_DUR_ETHEREAL_VISAGE, int nDurationType = DURATION_TYPE_TEMPORARY, float fDuration = 10.0f)
doc: THIS FUNCTION INTENDED FOR USE IN OnEntered SCRIPT FOR A TRIGGER.
Add a highlight to the nearest object with the same tag
as the trigger when a PC enters the trigger area.
See comments to AddHighlight for suggested highlight effects.

functions.name: TriggerRemoveHighlight
doc: THIS FUNCTION INTENDED FOR USE IN OnExited SCRIPT FOR A TRIGGER.
Remove the highlight from the nearest object with the same tag
as the trigger when a PC exits the trigger area.

functions.name: INF_CreateRandomEncounter
doc: Create a random encounter in the specified area, possibly based
on the PC's characteristics.

functions.name: INF_CreateRandomPlaceables
doc: Create random decorative placeables in the specified area.

functions.name: INF_GetEntryMessage
doc: Message sent to the player the first time they enter an
infinite area.

functions.name: INF_GetReentryMessage
doc: The message sent to the player when they re-enter an
infinite area that they've been to before.

functions.name: INF_GetReachStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they get dropped back to the starting
point from within the desert for the first time.

functions.name: INF_GetReturnToStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they get dropped back to the starting
point after the first time.

functions.name: INF_GetReachRewardMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they reach the reward area of the infinite
run for the first time.

functions.name: INF_GetReturnToRewardMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they reach the reward area of the infinite
run on a subsequent re-entry.

functions.name: INF_GetPoolEmptyMessage
doc: Message sent to PC in the case where the pool of generic areas
is empty and no new area can be allocated. This should happen
very rarely, and the result will be that the PC is returned to
the starting point.

functions.name: INF_GetNeedKeyMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they would reach the reward area but don't
have the key.

functions.name: INF_GetNoStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when no starting marker is available, which
means they can't enter the desert. This should only happen if
the modmaker has forgotten to put down a starting marker!

functions.name: PickRandomEncounter
doc: Randomly selects one of the encounter lists above

functions.name: CreateEncounterGroup
doc: This takes a string for an encounter type (eg, ENCOUNTER_WOLF)
that is a pipe-delimited list of creature blueprints. It will
randomly generate the specified number of creatures out of the
list and create them in the given location.
The last two entries in the list after the + symbol are the
min/max number of creatures to generate.
The last blueprint in a list is the boss. (To make a list
with no boss, simply repeat one creature at the end.) Bosses will
be unique and will show up randomly, if the given PC has HD lower
than the minimum hit dice required.
Each creature in the list will have an equal chance of showing up.
Each creature will have the ambient animations spawn-in condition
set to make them wander about.

functions.name: INF_CreateRandomEncounter
doc: Create a random encounter in the specified area, possibly based
on the PC's characteristics.
This particular version looks for a waypoint named
with the INF_ENCOUNTER tag and spawns there if it exists.

functions.name: INF_CreateRandomPlaceables
doc: Create some random decorative placeables in the specified area.

functions.name: INF_GetEntryMessage
doc: Message sent to the player the first time they enter an
infinite area.

functions.name: INF_GetReentryMessage
doc: The message sent to the player when they re-enter an
infinite area that they've been to before.

functions.name: INF_GetReachStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they get dropped back to the starting
point from within the desert for the first time or from the
reward area.

functions.name: INF_GetReturnToStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they get dropped back to the starting
point after the first time. This only gets sent if there is no
reward area.

functions.name: INF_GetReachRewardMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they reach the reward area of the infinite
run for the first time.
<CUSTOM0> in this message will be replaced by the name of the area.

functions.name: INF_GetReturnToRewardMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they reach the reward area of the infinite
run on a subsequent re-entry.
<CUSTOM0> in this message will be replaced by the name of the area.

functions.name: INF_GetPoolEmptyMessage
doc: Message sent to PC in the case where the pool of generic areas
is empty AND none of the generic areas currently allocated have
anyone from their same starting point inside. This should happen
very rarely, and the result will be that the PC is returned to
the starting point.

functions.name: INF_GetNeedKeyMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when they would reach the reward area but don't
have the key.

functions.name: INF_GetNoStartMessage
doc: Message sent to PC when no starting marker is available, which
means they can't enter the desert. This should only happen if
the modmaker has forgotten to put down a starting marker!

functions.name: VerifyDisarm
doc: Checks that the melee talent being used
is Disarm and if so then if the target has a
weapon.
This should return TRUE if:
- we are not trying to use disarm
- we are using disarm appropriately
This should return FALSE if:
- we are trying to use disarm on an inappropriate target
- we are using disarm too frequently
If this returns FALSE, we will fall back to a standard
melee attack instead.

functions.name: CreateReplacementNPC
functions.params: object oPC, string sNPCResRef, location lNPC
functions.signature: object CreateReplacementNPC(object oPC, string sNPCResRef, location lNPC)
doc: Create a replacement for the NPC
General system:
- Create an NPC *BLUEPRINT*
- Put it in a non-hostile faction and set it PLOT
- Use the script 'x0_d1_go_hostile' in conversation to cause the
NPC to become hostile and to unset its PLOT flag.
- Put the script 'x0_death_npc' in the OnDeath handler function
of the NPC.
- Create a conversation node for after the NPC's death using the
script 'x0_d2_npckilled'.
- Paint the NPC in the appropriate location.

functions.name: GetProjectileTrapOrigin
functions.signature: object GetProjectileTrapOrigin(object oTarget, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Get the origin for a projectile trap. This simply returns the nearest
object to the target that has a tag matching the tag of the trigger.

functions.name: TriggerProjectileTrap
functions.params: int nSpell, object oTarget, int nCasterLevel = 20, object oOrigin = OBJECT_INVALID, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nProjectilePath = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT
functions.signature: void TriggerProjectileTrap(int nSpell, object oTarget, int nCasterLevel = 20, object oOrigin = OBJECT_INVALID, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nProjectilePath = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT)
doc: Causes oOrigin to fire a specified spell (SPELL_*) at oTarget,
using the specified caster level (SEE *** COMMENTS).
The specified level doesn't work yet.
As a kludge, we are using the reflex save of the origin as
the caster level. This kludge only works for a few spells
at the moment. Other spells will fire typically at the
highest caster level allowed for that spell.
If the origin is invalid, the function will attempt to find the
object nearest the target that has the same tag as the trigger.
If no such object exists, we assume that the origin of the trap
has been destroyed, and don't fire the trap.
Only creatures, placeables, and items can be used as trap origins.
Note that a few spells (notably the arrow/bolt/dart/shuriken) require
PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_HOMING to work correctly.
See the projectile trap trigger blueprints for usage.

functions.name: GetIsTrapOriginValid

is a valid origin object.

functions.name: GetProjectileTrapOrigin
functions.signature: object GetProjectileTrapOrigin(object oTarget, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Get the origin for a projectile trap. This simply returns the nearest
object to the target that has a tag matching the tag of the trigger.

functions.name: TriggerProjectileTrap
functions.params: int nSpell, object oTarget, int nCasterLevel = 20, object oOrigin = OBJECT_INVALID, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nProjectilePath = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT
functions.signature: void TriggerProjectileTrap(int nSpell, object oTarget, int nCasterLevel = 20, object oOrigin = OBJECT_INVALID, object oTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nProjectilePath = PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT)
doc: See detailed comments above

functions.name: GetIsSecretItemRevealed

should be called from the detect trigger, not the item itself.
(If you have the item itself, it is definitely revealed!)

doc: Returns the associated secret item

doc: Returns whether the secret item is currently open --
should be called from the secret item itself after
being revealed.

functions.params: object oSecretItem = OBJECT_SELF, int bValue = TRUE
functions.signature: void SetIsSecretItemOpen(object oSecretItem = OBJECT_SELF, int bValue = TRUE)
doc: Set whether the secret item is open or not

functions.name: DetectSecretItem
functions.params: object oPC, object oDetectTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nSkillType = SKILL_SEARCH
functions.signature: int DetectSecretItem(object oPC, object oDetectTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nSkillType = SKILL_SEARCH)
doc: Detection function for a secret item.
Uses the specified skill (defaults to SKILL_SEARCH; use any of the
SKILL_ constants here) and uses the key tag of the trigger as the DC
of the detection check.
Returns TRUE if player detects, FALSE otherwise.

functions.name: DetectSecretItemByClass
functions.params: object oPC, object oDetectTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nClassType = CLASS_TYPE_RANGER
functions.signature: int DetectSecretItemByClass(object oPC, object oDetectTrigger = OBJECT_SELF, int nClassType = CLASS_TYPE_RANGER)
doc: Detection function that reveals an item only to members of the specified
class. Compares the PC's level in the class plus a small modifier against
the value of the key tag of the trigger.

functions.name: RevealSecretItem
functions.signature: void RevealSecretItem(string sResRef, object oDetectTrigger = OBJECT_SELF)
doc: Reveal function for a secret item. When called, this item
creates the secret item from blueprint in the location of the
item waypoint. Any item blueprint resref can be used here.
Some common items are the ones in the placeables palette listed
under "Secret Items".

functions.name: ResetSecretItem
doc: Reset function for a secret item. When called, this destroys the
item and resets the invisible detection object going again. This WILL
cause any treasure and locks/traps on the door to regenerate.

functions.name: TransformObjectToItem
functions.params: object oOrigin, string sItem = "", object oInventory = OBJECT_INVALID
functions.signature: void TransformObjectToItem(object oOrigin, string sItem = "", object oInventory = OBJECT_INVALID)
doc: This causes an object to be transformed into an item in the
specified inventory.
This is useful for special placeables,
where you want to be able to leave an "item" lying around
on the ground that doesn't look like a bag but still can be
picked up (without creating a new base item type).
This function would go in the "OnUsed" script for the
placeable, like this:
TransformObjectToItem(OBJECT_SELF, "blueprint of item", GetLastUsedBy());
If you leave the blueprint blank, the function will attempt to
use a blueprint formed by taking the resref of the origin and
tacking on "_i" -- so if you have a placeable called "hobbyhorse",
and you make an item with a blueprint "hobbyhorse_i", just use the
script "x0_o2_pickup" as the OnUsed of the hobbyhorse placeable.

functions.name: TravelToObject
functions.params: object oDest, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int bRun = FALSE, float fDelay = 10.0
functions.signature: void TravelToObject(object oDest, object oTarget = OBJECT_SELF, int bRun = FALSE, float fDelay = 10.0)
doc: Target goes to specified destination object intelligently.
If location is in same area, walk (or run) there.
If location is in different area, walk (or run) to
most appropriate door, area transition, or waypoint,
then jump.
If either of these fail, jump after fDelay seconds.

doc: Look up the caller's waypoints and store them on the creature.
Waypoint variables:

WN_NUM     : number of night waypoints
WP_#, WN_# : the waypoint objects
WP_CUR     : the current waypoint number
bCrossAreas: if set to TRUE, the creature will travel between areas to reach
its waypoint

functions.name: GetNextWalkWayPoint

If it has just become day/night, or if this is
the first time we're getting a waypoint, we go
to the nearest waypoint in our new set.

doc: Get the number of the nearest of the creature's current

functions.name: WalkWayPoints
functions.signature: void WalkWayPoints(int nRun = FALSE, float fPause = 1.0)
doc: HEAVILY REVISED!
The previous version of this function was too little
bang-for-the-buck, as it set up an infinite loop and
made creatures walk around even when there was no one
in the area.
Now, each time this function is called, the caller
will move to their next waypoint. The OnHeartbeat and
OnPerception scripts have been modified to call this
function as appropriate.
However, also note that the mobile ambient animations
have been heavily revised. For most creatures, those
should now be good enough, especially if you put down
some "NW_STOP" waypoints for them to wander among.
Specific waypoints will now be more for creatures that
you really want to patrol back and forth along a pre-set
path.

functions.name: GetCombatDifficulty
functions.params: object oRelativeTo = OBJECT_SELF, int bEnable = FALSE
functions.signature: int GetCombatDifficulty(object oRelativeTo = OBJECT_SELF, int bEnable = FALSE)
doc: Return the combat difficulty.
This is only used for henchmen and its only function currently
is to keep henchmen from casting spells in an easy fight.
This determines the difficulty by counting the number of allies
and enemies and their respective CRs, then converting the value
into a "spell CR" rating.
A value of 20 means use whatever you have, a negative value
means a very easy fight.

functions.name: bkAcquireTarget
doc: Determine our target for the next combat round.
Normally, this will be the same target as the last round.
The only time this changes is if the target is gone/killed
or they are in dying mode.

functions.name: ChooseNewTarget
doc: Choose a new nearby target. Target must be an enemy, perceived,
and not in dying mode. If possible, we first target members of
a class we "hate" -- this is generally random, to keep everyone
from attacking the same target.

functions.name: GetCRMax
doc: Determines the Spell CR to be used in the
given situation
BK: changed this. It returns the the max CR for
this particular scenario.
NOTE: Will apply to all creatures though it may
be necessary to limit it just for associates.
Created By: Preston Watamaniuk
Created On: Nov 18, 2001

functions.name: bkEvaluationSanityCheck

functions.name: bkTalentFilter
functions.params: talent tUse, object oTarget, int bJustTest = FALSE
functions.signature: int bkTalentFilter(talent tUse, object oTarget, int bJustTest = FALSE)
doc: This function is the last minute filter to prevent
any inappropriate effects from being applied
to inapproprite creatures.
Returns TRUE if the talent was valid, FALSE otherwise.
If an invalid talent is attempted, we instead perform
a standard melee attack to avoid AI stopping.
Based on Pausanias's Final Talent Filter.
Parameters
bJustTest = If this is true the function only does a test
the action stack is NOT modified at all

but very handy for cutscenes that are complete, but need to be changed

functions.name: CutRemoveEffects

functions.signature: void CutRemoveEffects(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to remove all effects from an object.
Example: CutRemoveEffects(10.5, GetObjectByTag("guard1")); would
remove all effects from guard1 after a 10.5 second delay.

functions.name: CutActionStartConversation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oNPC, object oPC, string szConversationFile, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionStartConversation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oNPC, object oPC, string szConversationFile, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used for a conversation file when you need to have an NPC speak
via a conversation instead of bubble text. The fDelay is used
when timing is important.
Example: CutActionStartConversation(5.0, oNPC, oPC, "my_conv"); would start the
conversation file "my_conv" of the NPC, after a 5 sec delay, and
the conversation subject would be the PC.

functions.name: CutSpeakString
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oSpeaker, string szString, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSpeakString(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oSpeaker, string szString, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used for bubble text type speaking.
Example: CutSpeakString(0.0, GetObjectByTag("drow_priest"), "I like
green eggs and ham."); would have the object drow_priest speak the
line "I like green eggs and ham." after no delay.

functions.name: CutPlayAnimation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutPlayAnimation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used if you need a cutscene participant to do an animation.
Example: CutPlayAnimation(<cutscene>, 26.0, oPC, ANIMATION_FIREFORGET_BOW, 3.0);
would have the PC bow for 3 seconds after a 26 second delay.

functions.name: CutPlayAnimation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutPlayAnimation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used if you need a cutscene participant to do an animation.
Example: CutPlayAnimation(<cutscene>, 26.0, oPC, ANIMATION_FIREFORGET_BOW, 3.0);
would have the PC bow for 3 seconds after a 26 second delay.

functions.name: CutJumpToLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, location lLoc, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutJumpToLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, location lLoc, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to jump the PC to a location.
Example: CutJumpToLocation(20.0, oPC, GetLocation(GetWaypointByTag
("wp_jump")); would jump the PC to the wp_jump waypoint after a 20
second delay.

functions.name: CutJumpToObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutJumpToObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to jump the PC or NPC to an object.
Example: CutJumpToObject(20.0, oPC, GetObject(GetObjectByTag
("invis_object")); would jump the PC to the invis_object after a 20
second delay.

functions.name: CutActionMoveToObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oTarget, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionMoveToObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oTarget, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to move the PC or NPC to an object.
Example: CutActionMoveToObject(2.0, oPC, oTable, TRUE); would have the
PC run to oTable after a 2 second delay. TRUE = run, FALSE = walk.

functions.name: CutActionMoveToLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, location lLoc, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionMoveToLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, location lLoc, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to move the PC or NPC to a location.
Example: CutActionMoveToLocation(2.0, oPC, lTable, FALSE); would have the
PC walk to lTable after a 2 second delay. TRUE = run, FALSE = walk.

functions.name: CutCreateObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iType, string sName, location lLoc, int iEffect, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutCreateObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iType, string sName, location lLoc, int iEffect, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to delay object create. Pass the type, the sTemplate, the location,
and the effect you wish to have appear when the object is created
(0 for no effect).  The PC is also passed to check cutscene abort.
EXAMPLE: CutCreateObject(2.3, oPC, OBJECT_TYPE_PLACEABLE, "resref", lLoc, VFX_FNF_HOLY_STRIKE);
would create a placeable with the resref of "resref" at the location
lLoc after 2.3 seconds, with the VFX_FNF_HOLY_STRIKE effect.

functions.name: CutSetFacingPoint
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, string szTag, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetFacingPoint(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, string szTag, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to set the facing of a creature.
EXAMPLE CutSetFacingPoint(22.5, oPC, "creature_tag"); would
have the PC face "creature_tag" object after a 22.5 second delay.

functions.name: CutFadeOutAndIn
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutFadeOutAndIn(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to fade out and back in, with a black screen section of
2 seconds in between.
EXAMPLE: CallFadeOutAndIn(35.0, oPC); would Fade the screen
out and back in on the PC, after a delay of 35 seconds.

functions.name: CutFadeToBlack
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutFadeToBlack(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to fade out.
EXAMPLE: CallFadeToBlack(35.0, oPC); would Fade the screen
out on the PC, after a delay of 35 seconds.

functions.name: CutFadeFromBlack
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutFadeFromBlack(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to fade in.
EXAMPLE: CallFadeFromBlack(35.0, oPC); would Fade the screen
in on the PC, after a delay of 35 seconds.

functions.name: CutSetCamera
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iCameraType, float fFacing, float fZoom, float fPitch, int nSpeed, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetCamera(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iCameraType, float fFacing, float fZoom, float fPitch, int nSpeed, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to set the camera for cutscene dramatics.
EXAMPLE: To set a camera with the following settings on the PC,
after a 30.0 second delay:
CAMERA_MODE_TOP_DOWN
Facing = 170.0
Zoom = 5.0
Pitch = 50.0
CAMERA_TRANSITION_TYPE_MEDIUM
CutSetCamera(30.0, oPC, CAMERA_MODE_TOP_DOWN, 170.0, 5.0, 50.0,
CAMERA_TRANSITION_TYPE_MEDIUM);

functions.name: CutClearAllActions
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutClearAllActions(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to clear the actions of the subject.
EXAMPLE: CutClearAllActions(3.2, GetObjectByTag("guard"));
would clear the actions of "guard" after a 3.2 second delay.

functions.name: CutApplyEffectAtLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iDur, int iEffect, location lLoc, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutApplyEffectAtLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iDur, int iEffect, location lLoc, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to apply a visual effect at a location. The PC is passed to
determine if the cutscene has been aborted or not.
EXAMPLE: CutApplyEffectAtLocation(98.3, oPC, DURATION_TYPE_INSTANT,
VXF_FNF_HOLY_STRIKE, lLoc, 0.0);
would have a holy strike visual appear at lLoc after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is instant, lasting the default time.

functions.name: CutApplyEffectToObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iDur, int iEffect, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutApplyEffectToObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iDur, int iEffect, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to apply a visual effect to an object.
EXAMPLE: CutApplyEffectToObject(98.3, oPC, DURATION_TYPE_TEMPORARY,
VXF_DUR_PETRIFY, oPC, 4.0);
would have a PETRIFY visual appear to the PC after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is temporary, lasting 4 seconds.

functions.name: CutApplyEffectToObject2
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iDur, effect eEffect, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutApplyEffectToObject2(int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iDur, effect eEffect, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: For all other effects (NOT VISUAL EFFECTS)
Used to apply a NON visual effect to an object.

functions.name: CutKnockdown
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutKnockdown(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, float fDur, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to apply a knockdown effect to an object.
EXAMPLE: CutKnockdown(98.3, oPC, 4.0);
would have a knockdown appear on the PC after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is always temporary, this one lasting 4 seconds.

functions.name: CutDeath
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iSpec, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutDeath(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iSpec, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to apply a death effect to an object.
EXAMPLE: CutDeath(98.3, oPC, TRUE);
would have a death appear on the PC after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is always instant. TRUE is used if you want a
spectacular death. Set FALSE otherwise.

functions.name: CutActionCastFakeSpellAtObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iSpell, object oObject, object oTarget, int iPath, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionCastFakeSpellAtObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iSpell, object oObject, object oTarget, int iPath, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to cast a fake spell at an object.
EXAMPLE: CutActionCastFakeSpellAtObject(23.0, oPC, SPELL_SUNBEAM, oTarget,
PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT); would have the
PC cast a sunbeam at the oTarget, with a default
projectile path. This would happen after a 23
second delay.

functions.name: CutActionCastFakeSpellAtLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iSpell, object oObject, location lLoc, int iPath, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionCastFakeSpellAtLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, int iSpell, object oObject, location lLoc, int iPath, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to cast a fake spell at a location.
EXAMPLE: CutActionCastFakeSpellAtLocation(23.0, oPC, SPELL_SUNBEAM, lLoc,
PROJECTILE_PATH_TYPE_DEFAULT); would have the
PC cast a sunbeam at lLoc, with a default
projectile path. This would happen after a 23
second delay.

functions.name: CutSetLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to set the PC's location so they can be jumped around as though
they are the camera.
EXAMPLE: CutSetLocation(23.0, oPC); would set the location
on the PC, as a variable, as a location to be returned to later. This
would happen after a 23 second delay.

functions.name: CutRestoreLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutRestoreLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to recall the PC's location so they can be jumped back to their
original location, stored by CutGetLocation.
EXAMPLE: CutRestoreLocation(23.0, oPC); would return the PC
to their original spot. This would happen after a 23 second delay.

functions.name: CutSetCutsceneMode
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iValue, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetCutsceneMode(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iValue, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to turn cutscene mode on or off.
EXAMPLE: CutSetCutsceneMode(10.0, oPC, TRUE); would turn cutscene mode
on after 10 seconds for the PC.

functions.name: CutSetPlotFlag
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iValue, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetPlotFlag(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iValue, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to turn plot flags on or off.
EXAMPLE: CutSetPlotFlag(10.0, oObject, 1); would turn plot flag to
on after 10 seconds for the object.

functions.name: CutDestroyObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutDestroyObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to destroy objects. Make sure they are destroyable first with the
CutSetPlotFlag function.
EXAMPLE: CutDestroyObject(10.0, oObject); would destroy the object
after 10 seconds.

functions.name: CutStoreCameraFacing
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutStoreCameraFacing(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to store the camera position.
EXAMPLE: CutStoreCameraFacing(10.0, oPC); would set the current
camera settings at the 10.0 mark.

functions.name: CutRestoreCameraFacing
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutRestoreCameraFacing(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to restore the camera position.
EXAMPLE: CutRestoreCameraFacing(10.0, oPC); would set the old
camera settings back at the 10.0 mark.

functions.name: CutBlackScreen
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutBlackScreen(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to set the screen to black.  This is very useful in covering up
and cutscene jumps that happen in the same area as the play area.
EXAMPLE: CutBlackScreen(10.0, oPC); would set the screen to black
at the 10.0 mark.

functions.name: CutStopFade
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutStopFade(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to remove the screen to black.
EXAMPLE: CutStopFade(10.0, oPC); would set the screen back from black
at the 10.0 mark.

functions.name: GetShift
doc: Used to shift the entire cutscene a length of time. Used internally here,
but very handy for cutscenes that are complete, but need to be changed
after the fact. From the point it is called, it just shifts the cutscene
accordingly. Can be used (and should be) for non-cutscene delays in a
script (ie - commands that aren't here or regular DelayCommands in a script.)

functions.name: RemoveCommandable
doc: Used to clear commandable state FALSE in the event there are too many
Used in this include only.

functions.name: CallRemoveEffects
doc: Private function used to implement the same function with the "Cut" prefix in
place of the "Call" prefix.

functions.name: CutRemoveEffects
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutRemoveEffects(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to remove all effects from an object.
Example: CutRemoveEffects(10.5, GetObjectByTag("guard1")); would
remove all effects from guard1 after a 10.5 second delay.

functions.params: int nCutscene, object oNPC, object oPC, string szConversationFile
functions.signature: void CallActionStartConversation(int nCutscene, object oNPC, object oPC, string szConversationFile)

functions.params: int nCutscene, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength
functions.signature: void CallPlayAnimation(int nCutscene, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength)
doc: Private function used to implement CutPlayAnimation.

functions.name: CutPlayAnimation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int nAnimation, float fLength, int iShift = TRUE

functions.signature: void CallActionMoveToObject(int nCutscene, object oPC, object oTarget, int iRun)
doc: Private function used to implement the same function with the "Cut" prefix in

functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oTarget, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutActionMoveToObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, object oTarget, int iRun, int iShift = TRUE)

functions.name: CutCreateObject
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iType, string sName, location lLoc, int iEffect, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutCreateObject(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oPC, int iType, string sName, location lLoc, int iEffect, int iShift = TRUE)

functions.name: CallApplyEffectAtLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, object oObject, int iDur, int iEffect, location lLoc, float fDur

doc: Private function used to implement the same function with the "Cut" prefix in
place of the "Call" prefix.

functions.name: CutApplyEffectAtLocation
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iDur, int iEffect, location lLoc, float fDur = 0.0, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutApplyEffectAtLocation(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iDur, int iEffect, location lLoc, float fDur = 0.0, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to apply a visual effect at a location. The PC is passed to
determine if the cutscene has been aborted or not.
EXAMPLE: CutApplyEffectAtLocation(98.3, oPC, DURATION_TYPE_INSTANT,
VXF_FNF_HOLY_STRIKE, lLoc, 0.0);
would have a holy strike visual appear at lLoc after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is instant, lasting the default time.

doc: Used to apply a visual effect to an object.
EXAMPLE: CutApplyEffectToObject(98.3, oPC, DURATION_TYPE_TEMPORARY,
VXF_DUR_PETRIFY, oPC, 4.0);
would have a PETRIFY visual appear to the PC after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is temporary, lasting 4 seconds.

functions.name: CallApplyEffectToObject2

functions.signature: void CallApplyEffectToObject2(int nCutscene, int iDur, effect eEffect, object oObject, float fDur)

Used to apply a NON visual effect to an object.

functions.name: CutKnockdown
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, float fDur = 3.0, int iShift = TRUE

EXAMPLE: CutKnockdown(98.3, oPC, 4.0);

functions.signature: void CutDeath(int nCutscene, float fDelay, object oObject, int iSpec = FALSE, int iShift = TRUE)

EXAMPLE: CutDeath(98.3, oPC, TRUE);
would have a death appear on the PC after a 98.3 second delay.
The duration is always instant. TRUE is used if you want a
spectacular death. Set FALSE otherwise.

they are the camera.
EXAMPLE: CutSetLocation(23.0, oPC); would set the location
on the PC, as a variable, as a location to be returned to later. This
would happen after a 23 second delay.

doc: Used to recall the PC's location so they can be jumped back to their
original location, stored by CutGetLocation.
EXAMPLE: CutRestoreLocation(23.0, oPC); would return the PC
to their original spot. This would happen after a 23 second delay.

functions.name: CutSetActiveCutsceneForObject
functions.params: object oObject, int nCutNum, int bMainPC = FALSE
functions.signature: int CutSetActiveCutsceneForObject(object oObject, int nCutNum, int bMainPC = FALSE)
doc: Use this function at the begining of a cutscene for each object involved in the cutscene.
Notice that this function would fail if oObject already has another cutscene value (any
number greater than 0). The function returns 0 on success, -1 otherwise.

functions.name: CutSetActiveCutscene
functions.params: int nCutsceneNumber, int nDelayType, int bSetCutsceneObject = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetActiveCutscene(int nCutsceneNumber, int nDelayType, int bSetCutsceneObject = TRUE)
doc: Set the current active cutscene. Any following delayed Cut* functions would run only if the
initiating object has the same cutscene number as the global active one. A value of -1
means that no cutscene is active (possible values for bDelayType: CUT_DELAY_TYPE_CUMULATIVE, CUT_DELAY_TYPE_CONSTANT.
bSetCutsceneObject should be set to FALSE whenever calling this function more than once for the
same cutscene but in another file. This would keep the original cutscene object that created
all the cutscene effect so it would be possible to remove them when the cutscene ends.

functions.name: GetActiveCutsceneNum
doc: Returns current global cutscene number.

functions.signature: void CutRemoveEffects(float fDelay, object oObject, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to remove all effects from an object.
Example: CutRemoveEffects(10.5, GetObjectByTag("guard1")) would remove all effects from guard1 after a 10.5 second delay.
Notice that only effects created for the cutscene would be removed.

functions.signature: void CutActionStartConversation(float fDelay, object oNPC, object oPC, string szConversationFile, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used for a conversation file when you need to have an NPC speak
via a conversation instead of bubble text. The fDelay is used
when timing is important.
Example: CutActionStartConversation(5.0, oNPC, oPC, "my_conv"); would start the
conversation file "my_conv" of the NPC, after a 5 sec delay, and
the conversation subject would be the PC.

functions.name: CutSetCutsceneMode
functions.params: float fDelay, object oPC, int iValue, int bInv, int bKeepAssociates = TRUE, int bFreezeAssociates = TRUE, int iShift = TRUE
functions.signature: void CutSetCutsceneMode(float fDelay, object oPC, int iValue, int bInv, int bKeepAssociates = TRUE, int bFreezeAssociates = TRUE, int iShift = TRUE)
doc: Used to turn cutscene mode on or off.
iValue: TRUE to start cutscene, and FALSE to exit cutscene mode.
bInv: TRUE -  to make the player invisible.
FASLE - would leave the player visible.
CUT_CAMERA_HEIGHT_VERY_LOW - would turn the playe invisible and put the camera at a very low position.
CUT_CAMERA_HEIGHT_LOW - would turn the playe invisible and put the camera at a low position.
CUT_CAMERA_HEIGHT_MEDIUM - would turn the playe invisible and put the camera at a low position.
CUT_CAMERA_HEIGHT_HIGH would turn the playe invisible and put the camera at a high position.
CUT_CAMERA_HEIGHT_VERY_HIGH would turn the playe invisible and put the camera at a very high position.
bKeepAssociates: would destroy all associate when set to FALSE.
bFreezeAssociates would freeze all associates when set to TRUE, and turn them invisible when set to VANISH = 2.

functions.name: CutSetAbortDelay
doc: Sets the delay between pressing the ESC key to actually doing the cutscene cleanup.
This function should be used at the beginning of a cutscene only if such delay is required.
Otherwise, there would be no cleanup delay.

doc: Retrieves the current abort delay for nCutscene after aborting.

functions.name: CutSetDestroyCopyDelay
doc: Sets the delay between pressing the ESC key to actually destroying the PC copy.
This function should be used at the beginning of a cutscene only if such delay is required.
Otherwise, there would be no delay.

functions.name: CutDisableCutscene
functions.params: int nCutscene, float fCleanupDelay, float fDestPCCopyDelay, int nRestoreType = RESTORE_TYPE_NORMAL
functions.signature: void CutDisableCutscene(int nCutscene, float fCleanupDelay, float fDestPCCopyDelay, int nRestoreType = RESTORE_TYPE_NORMAL)
doc: Disables a cutscene. All generic disable functions would be called after a delay of fCleanupDelay,
and any PC copy object would be destroyed after a delay of fDestPCCopyDelay).
If using the CUT_DELAY_TYPE_CUMULATIVE delay type, then each delay should be in relation to
the previous delay (independent delays). nRestoreType should have one of the following values:
- RESTORE_TYPE_NONE: Do not jump the player.
- RESTORE_TYPE_NORMAL: Jump the player to the last place that he used CutSetLocation() at.
- RESTORE_TYPE_COPY: Jupm the player to the place where the copy was created.

functions.name: CutDisableAbort
doc: Disables the possibility to disable nCutscene. Pressing ESC would do nothing after calling this function.

functions.name: CutGetIsAbortDisabled
doc: returns TRUE if it is not possible to abort nCutscene, FALSE otherwise.

functions.name: IPSafeAddItemProperty
functions.params: object oItem, itemproperty ip, float fDuration = 0.0f, int nAddItemPropertyPolicy = X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING, int bIgnoreDurationType = FALSE, int bIgnoreSubType = FALSE
functions.signature: void IPSafeAddItemProperty(object oItem, itemproperty ip, float fDuration = 0.0f, int nAddItemPropertyPolicy = X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING, int bIgnoreDurationType = FALSE, int bIgnoreSubType = FALSE)
doc: Add an item property in a safe fashion, preventing unwanted stacking
Parameters:
oItem     - the item to add the property to
ip        - the itemproperty to add
fDuration - set 0.0f to add the property permanent, anything else is temporary
nAddItemPropertyPolicy - How to handle existing properties. Valid values are:
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING - remove any property of the same type, subtype, durationtype before adding;
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_KEEP_EXISTING - do not add if any property with same type, subtype and durationtype already exists;
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_IGNORE_EXISTING - add itemproperty in any case - Do not Use with OnHit or OnHitSpellCast props!
bIgnoreDurationType - If set to TRUE, an item property will be considered identical even if the DurationType is different. Be careful when using this
with X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING, as this could lead to a temporary item property removing a permanent one
bIgnoreSubType      - If set to TRUE an item property will be considered identical even if the SubType is different.

functions.name: IPGetItemHasProperty
functions.params: object oItem, itemproperty ipCompareTo, int nDurationType, int bIgnoreSubType = FALSE
functions.signature: int IPGetItemHasProperty(object oItem, itemproperty ipCompareTo, int nDurationType, int bIgnoreSubType = FALSE)
doc: Wrapper for GetItemHasItemProperty that returns true if
oItem has an itemproperty that matches ipCompareTo by Type AND DurationType AND SubType
nDurationType = Valid DURATION_TYPE_* or -1 to ignore
bIgnoreSubType - If set to TRUE an item property will be considered identical even if the SubType is different.

Parameters:
oItem     - the item to add the property to
ip        - the itemproperty to add
fDuration - set 0.0f to add the property permanent, anything else is temporary
nAddItemPropertyPolicy - How to handle existing properties. Valid values are:
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING - remove any property of the same type, subtype, durationtype before adding;
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_KEEP_EXISTING - do not add if any property with same type, subtype and durationtype already exists;
X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_IGNORE_EXISTING - add itemproperty in any case - Do not Use with OnHit or OnHitSpellCast props!
bIgnoreDurationType  - If set to TRUE, an item property will be considered identical even if the DurationType is different. Be careful when using this
with X2_IP_ADDPROP_POLICY_REPLACE_EXISTING, as this could lead to a temporary item property removing a permanent one
bIgnoreSubType       - If set to TRUE an item property will be considered identical even if the SubType is different.
WARNING: This function is used all over the game. Touch it and break it and the wrath
of the gods will come down on you faster than you can saz "I didn't do it"

functions.name: WMSetupAmbush
doc: Setup the necessary variables for the x2_restsys_ambus script
You probably won't ever need this function as it is used internally by the system, but you could
use it to build your own monster ambush (just call WMRunAmbush() to execute)
oPC - The player to be ambushed
sMonsters - one or more monster ResRefs, comma seperated with no whitespaces in between
Examples:
WMSetupAmbush(GetEnteringObject(), "nw_dog,nw_dog") for multiple enemies
WMSetupAmbush(GetEnteringObject(), "nw_badger") for a single enemy

functions.name: WMCheckForWanderingMonster
doc: Georg Zoeller,  2003-05-29
Wandering Monster System, Check for wandering
monster
oPC - the player who triggered the check
This will check if the player has triggered a
wandering monster and return TRUE if yes.
To be used in the OnRest event handler.
It will also setup the encounter so if

more than one encounter in X2_WM_FLOODPROTPERIOD
seconds (see header)

Darkness (Drow/Drider) : 1+ 1 use per 5 levels
Stonegaze(Medusa) :      1+ 1 use per 5 levels
Stonegaze(Basilisk) :    1+ 1 use per 5 levels
Stonegaze(Basilisk) :    1+ 1 use per 5 levels
MindBlast(Illithid) :    1+ 1 use per 3 levels
Domination(Vampire) :    1+ 1 use per 5 levels

oItem - pass GetSpellTargetObject in here

functions.name: X2GetSpellCastOnSequencerItem

Returns TRUE (and charges the sequencer item) if the spell
... was cast on an item AND
... the item has the sequencer property
... the spell was non hostile
... the spell was not cast from an item
in any other case, FALSE is returned an the normal spellscript will be run
oItem - pass GetSpellTargetObject in here

functions.name: X2CastOnItemWasAllowed
doc: GZ: This is a filter I added to prevent spells from firing their original spell
script when they were cast on items and do not have special coding for that
case. If you add spells that can be cast on items you need to put them into
des_crft_spells.2da

(and all craft item code) from being executed.

doc: Define the name of the 2da file which is used for the wandering monster
system

functions.name: DTSGenerateTreasureOnContainer
functions.params: object oContainer, object oOpener, int nClass, int nType = 5
functions.signature: void DTSGenerateTreasureOnContainer(object oContainer, object oOpener, int nClass, int nType = 5)
doc: Generate random, disposeable treasure on the container
oContainer - Valid object with inventory
oOpener    - The one who opened the container
nClass - Treasure Class (X2_DTS_CLASS_*)
nType  - Treasure Type  (X2_DTS_TYPE_*), default = X2_DTS_TYPE_DISPOSEABLE | X2_DTS_TYPE_GOLD
Values: X2_DTS_TYPE_DISPOSEABLE - Potions, Kits, etc
X2_DTS_TYPE_AMMO        - Ammunition
X2_DTS_TYPE_GOLD        - Gold and Gems
X2_DTS_TYPE_ITEM    - Character Optimized treasure (ignores treasure class)
Example:
Generate Low Class Ammo and Gold+Gems
DTSGenerateTreasureOnContainer (oChest, X2_DTS_CLASS_LOW, X2_DTS_TYPE_AMMO | X2_DTS_TYPE_GOLD);

functions.name: DTSGenerateCharSpecificTreasure
functions.params: object oContainer, object oAdventurer, int bIgnoreFeats = FALSE
functions.signature: object DTSGenerateCharSpecificTreasure(object oContainer, object oAdventurer, int bIgnoreFeats = FALSE)
doc: Generates one random, character specific item on container
Treasure is optimized to suit a characters needs
if bIgnoreFeats is set TRUE, the system will not use Feats (i.e. Weapon Focus)
to determine a baseitem to spawn

functions.name: DTSInitialize
doc: Initializes the treasure system by loading x2_conf_tras.2da

functions.name: DTSGetStackVariation

If no configuration is used, default is X2_DTS_STACKVAR (0.5)
Stacks are calculated (Stack* X2_DTS_STACKVAR) + Random (Stack *X2_DTS_STACKVAR)
